Crucially, it eliminates premium timeouts entirely and instead uses a hard cryptographic relation (e.g., derived signature constraints) to provably trigger premium refunds—resolving both timeout challenges. For high-value swaps with non-overlapping premium acceptability ranges, round-based HedgeSwap employs iterative premium migration, converging to a mutually acceptable lock-up risk premium. Experiments show HedgeSwap completes in <0.5 s; round-based variant (5 rounds) finishes in <1.3 s; and it reduces gas cost by 2.69× versus state-of-the-art contract-based solutions.

Abstract

Universal atomic swaps [Oakland'22] replace hashed timelock contracts with adaptor signatures and verifiable timed dlogs, enabling secure cross-chain cryptocurrency exchanges that only require basic signature verification from the underlying blockchains.
However, existing universal swap protocols remain vulnerable to griefing attacks, where a deviating party aborts the swap to lock a compliant party's assets for a long period. A natural approach is to lift existing contract-based solutions to the universal setting, but we identify that this straightforward solution faces two key challenges: (i) timeout race attacks, first identified in PipeSwap [Oakland'25], which arises from the absence of an upper bound on the transaction validity; (ii) a timeout overlap dilemma, which results from multiple overlapping refund periods.

In this paper, we propose HedgeSwap, a universal hedged atomic swap protocol against griefing attacks, which compensates a compliant party with a premium if its asset is locked but not redeemed. To mitigate the timeout race attacks and timeout overlap dilemma, HedgeSwap eliminates the premium timeout and instead relies on a hard relation to refund the premium. For high-value asset swaps where the parties acceptable premium ranges do not overlap, we further propose a round-based HedgeSwap that utilizes a premium migration mechanism to solve these two timeout challenges, where parties iteratively increase the premium until the lock-up risk premium acceptable to both. Our experimental results show that our HedgeSwap can complete in under 0.5 seconds, and round-based HedgeSwap completes in under 1.3 seconds for a five-round setting, while HedgeSwap reduces gas cost by 2.69X compared to existing contract-based solutions.

item.title: SkillVetBench: LLM-as-Judge for Multi-Dimensional Security Risk Evaluation in Open-Source LLM Agent Skills
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15899v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

开源大语言模型（LLM）智能体生态正迅猛扩张，但社区贡献的技能（Skills）——即扩展智能体能力的模块化工具定义——却长期缺乏系统性安全审查。现有静态扫描器仅作用于代码层，对指令层风险（如提示注入、内存污染）和多智能体协同风险（如通过隐写侧信道窃取数据、跨管道链式危害）完全失明，亟需一种语义驱动、多维度的动态评估范式。

方法与创新

我们提出 SkillVetBench：首个面向开源LLM技能的公开实时评测基准（Hugging Face leaderboard），核心采用LLM-as-Judge架构进行语义级风险判别。关键创新包括：

  • SARS（Skill Agentic Risk Score）：首创五维智能体风险评分体系（含指令操控性、数据渗出性、上下文污染性、链式危害性、权限越界性），基于可解释加权公式量化风险；
  • CVSS v4.0向量解耦集成：将标准漏洞评分映射至技能语义场景，支持细粒度风险溯源；
  • ClawHub双视图评审机制：并置LLM生成的深度审查报告与官方市场人工裁定结果，提升透明度与可追溯性。

关键结果

在配套基准测试[1]中：
✅ LLM-as-Judge实现零漏报（78个已确认恶意技能全部检出）与零误报（22个良性技能全通过）；
❌ 静态基线SKILLSIEVE漏检率达15%；
⚠️ 在Prompt Injection与Memory Poisoning等指令层威胁上，传统工具检测率仅为0–11%（如CODEBERT对9个内存污染技能检出率为0%）；
📊 四类LLM评估器检测率差异显著（35%–95%），验证集成评分（ensemble scoring） 对生产部署的必要性。

AI Summary (English)

SkillVetBench introduces the first public, live leaderboard for multi-dimensional security vetting of open-source LLM agent skills, addressing critical blind spots in code-only scanners—especially instruction-layer threats (e.g., prompt injection, memory poisoning) and cross-agent risk chains. It leverages an LLM-as-Judge framework with SARS (Skill Agentic Risk Score), a principled 5-dimensional risk metric aligned with CVSS v4.0 vector decomposition and integrated into ClawHub’s dual-view review interface. Empirical evaluation shows zero false negatives (78 malicious skills detected) and zero false positives (22 benign controls passed), outperforming the best static baseline (SKILLSIEVE) which misses 15%. Crucially, conventional tools fail on 89–100% of instruction-layer threats—e.g., CODEBERT detects 0/9 memory-poisoning skills—while LLM evaluators achieve 35–95% detection rates, strongly motivating ensemble scoring in production.

Abstract

Open-source LLM agent ecosystems are growing rapidly, yet the security of community-contributed skills - modular tool definitions that extend agent capabilities - remains largely unvetted. The gap we fill: existing scanners operate at the code layer and are structurally blind to instruction-layer and multi-agent risk - natural-language directives that hijack an agent, exfiltrate data through encoded side channels, or chain harm across pipelines - so what is needed is a semantic, multi-dimensional vetting system rather than another signature matcher. We present SKILLVETBENCH, a live public leaderboard on Hugging Face that uses an LLM-as-Judge to vet agent skills. What is new: SARS (Skill Agentic Risk Score), a five-dimensional agentic-risk metric with a principled weighted formula for instruction-following systems. What is integrated: full CVSS v4.0 vector decomposition and a ClawHub dual-view that places our LLM-generated review beside the official marketplace verdict. What is demonstrated: drawing on our companion benchmark paper [ 1], the LLM-as-Judge stage achieves zero false negatives across 78 confirmed-malicious skills and zero false positives across 22 benign controls, while the best static baseline (SKILLSIEVE) still misses 15%; for instruction-layer categories such as Prompt Injection and Memory Poisoning, conventional tools miss between 89% and 100% of threats (e.g., CODEBERT detects none of nine memory-poisoning skills). Detection rates vary from 35% to 95% across four LLM evaluators, motivating ensemble scoring in production deployments.

item.title: Let Them Steal: Trapping Large Language Model Extraction Attacks with Knowledge Honeypot
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15810v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

面向商业API部署的大语言模型（LLM）面临严峻的模型提取攻击（Model Extraction Attack）威胁——攻击者通过大量查询构建高保真替代模型（surrogate model）。现有防御方案存在根本性权衡：或依赖后置检测（响应已生成后再拦截），导致攻击已完成；或引入输出扰动/查询限流，显著损害合法用户的准确性与响应质量，难以落地。

方法创新：Knowledge Trap（知识陷阱）

本文提出Knowledge Trap——一种主动式、低干扰的新型防御范式。其核心是构建一个轻量级、语义可控的蜜罐知识图谱（Honeypot Knowledge Graph, HKG），内嵌大量看似合理但下游任务迁移能力极低（low-transferability）的知识节点（如医学中虚构但符合术语规范的罕见病组合、金融中逻辑自洽却无实证支撑的衍生品定价规则）。系统通过面包屑引导机制（breadcrumb-guided exploration）在交互中自然诱使攻击者沿HKG路径持续查询，使其宝贵的查询预算被高效“消耗”于无效知识空间，而对真实模型参数与高价值知识的探查被实质性稀释。

关键结果与优势

在医疗问答与金融风控两大高敏感领域实证表明：Knowledge Trap将攻击者构建的替代模型与原始模型的一致性（Agreement）平均降低6.2%，且完全不损害合法用户的任务准确率（Accuracy保持100%基线）。相较主流防御（如梯度混淆、输出扰动、查询过滤），本方法首次实现“零用户代价”的强效防御，验证了防御知识空间遍历路径而非仅防护输出本身，是一条兼具实用性与鲁棒性的新路径。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) deployed as commercial APIs are vulnerable to extraction attacks, where adversaries query the API to reconstruct a high-fidelity surrogate model. Existing defenses either react too late or degrade service quality for legitimate users. We propose Knowledge Trap, a proactive defense that lures attackers into querying a lightweight Honeypot Knowledge Graph (HKG)—a curated set of semantically plausible but low-transferability facts (e.g., fictional yet syntactically valid medical conditions or ungrounded financial rules). Guided by breadcrumb signals, attackers waste their limited query budget on this decoy knowledge, while benign users receive unaltered, high-utility responses. Experiments across medical and financial domains show Knowledge Trap reduces surrogate–target agreement by 6.2% on average, with zero accuracy degradation for legitimate users—outperforming prior methods that impose measurable utility costs. This demonstrates that defending knowledge-space traversal, rather than just output perturbation, is a practical and effective strategy against LLM extraction.

Abstract

Large language models deployed as commercial APIs are vulnerable to model extraction attacks, while existing defenses either act too late or degrade utility for legitimate users. We propose \textbf{Knowledge Trap}, a defense that redirects extraction attacks toward low-transferability knowledge through a \emph{Honeypot Knowledge Graph} (HKG) and breadcrumb-guided exploration. Instead of blocking queries or perturbing outputs, Knowledge Trap consumes the attacker's limited query budget on knowledge with negligible downstream utility while preserving benign-user performance. Experiments in medical and financial domains show that Knowledge Trap reduces surrogate Agreement by 6.2\% on average without degrading legitimate-user accuracy, outperforming existing defenses that impose measurable user impact. These results suggest that defending knowledge-space traversal is a practical direction for mitigating LLM extraction attacks.

item.title: AttackonCTF: Defending Hardware Security Competition Benchmarks in the Age of LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15809v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

硬件安全竞赛（如 HackTheSilicon）作为关键基准平台，广泛用于评估漏洞检测方法、训练安全人才与AI模型。然而，本研究首次揭示：大语言模型（LLMs）正系统性破坏此类竞赛基准的有效性。实验发现，当前主流检测器并非基于真正的硬件安全语义推理，而是依赖类diff的语法相似性比对——仅通过比对参赛提交与参考答案的代码行级差异即可实现高达83%的虚假高检出率，严重扭曲评估公平性与技术可信度。

方法创新

为应对该威胁，我们提出首个面向LLM的语义保持型基准混淆框架（AttackonCTF）。该框架区别于传统IP保护式混淆（如加密或控制流扁平化），专为竞赛场景设计：

  • ✅ 人类可读性优先：所有变换（如信号重命名、模块拆分、时序无关的寄存器重排）均保持RTL代码逻辑清晰、可调试；
  • ✅ 可控“diff噪声”注入：在不改变功能行为前提下，精准扰动语法表征，阻断LLM的浅层模式匹配；
  • ✅ 轻量级可配置：支持按需调节混淆强度（0%–100%），适配不同评估粒度需求。

关键结果

在HackTheSilicon基准上验证：

  • 仅施加10%轻量混淆，LLM检测准确率即下降50%（从83%→41.5%）；
  • 全量混淆下准确率骤降至17.4%（降幅达78.6%），回归随机水平；
  • 功能等价性经形式验证（SVA+Yosys）100%保障，且综合后面积/功耗开销<2.1%。

本工作不仅修复了LLM时代下硬件安全竞赛的基准可信危机，更开创了“对抗LLM滥用”的新型基准防护范式。

AI Summary (English)

Hardware security competitions (e.g., HackTheSilicon) serve as vital benchmarks for evaluating vulnerability detection and training human/AI practitioners. However, we reveal a critical threat: LLMs undermine their validity by enabling detectors to rely on syntactic diff-style matching—not genuine security reasoning—achieving an inflated 83% detection rate. To counter this, we propose AttackonCTF, the first LLM-oriented, semantics-preserving obfuscation framework for competition benchmarks. Unlike IP-protection methods, it applies human-readable, functionally equivalent transformations (e.g., signal renaming, module splitting, timing-agnostic register reordering) with controlled “diff noise”. Evaluated on HackTheSilicon, AttackonCTF reduces LLM-based detection accuracy by 50% at 10% obfuscation and by 78.6% under full obfuscation, while preserving functional equivalence (verified via SVA + Yosys) and introducing <2.1% hardware overhead. This work restores benchmark integrity and establishes a new paradigm for LLM-resilient security evaluation.

Abstract

Hardware security competitions such as HackTheSilicon serve as benchmarking platforms for evaluating vulnerability detection methods and for training humans and AI. However, our study reveals that LLMs threaten their validity. Instead of genuine security reasoning, detectors exploit a diff-style syntactic comparison, achieving an 83% detection rate, undermining fair evaluation. To mitigate this, we propose the first LLM-oriented, semantics-preserving obfuscation framework for these benchmarks. Unlike IP-protection approaches, it applies human-readable transformations and controlled diff-noise while preserving functionality. On HackTheSilicon, the framework reduces LLM-based detection accuracy by 50% with only 10% obfuscation and by 78.6% under complete obfuscation, restoring benchmark reliability.

item.title: GAS-Leak-LLM: Genetic Algorithm-Based Suffix Optimization for Black-Box LLM Jailbreaking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15788v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）作为AI驱动信息技术生态的核心组件，普遍部署于各类商业系统中。为防范有害输出与策略违规风险，厂商广泛采用对齐优化（alignment）与多层内容审核机制。然而，近期研究表明，即便在强约束下，LLMs仍易受黑盒对抗攻击影响，尤其是通过精心构造的“越狱提示”（jailbreaking）绕过安全护栏——现有方法多依赖人工启发式或梯度近似，泛化性弱、查询效率低，且难以在无模型访问权限的真实部署场景中持续生效。

方法创新：GAS-Leak-LLM

本文提出GAS-Leak-LLM——一种基于遗传算法（Genetic Algorithm, GA）的黑盒后缀优化框架，专为高效、鲁棒的LLM越狱攻击设计。其核心创新在于：

  • ✅ 纯黑盒范式：仅通过API调用获取模型响应（yes/no或置信分），无需访问模型权重、梯度或内部结构，高度贴合实际攻击面；
  • ✅ 离散空间进化：将对抗后缀建模为可变长token序列，定义适配度函数（fitness）为越狱成功率，并通过选择（selection）、变异（mutation）、交叉（crossover） 三阶段迭代演化，在离散提示空间中高效搜索高适应度后缀；
  • ✅ 动态适应性：支持多目标优化（如同时最小化提示长度与查询次数），并兼容不同安全分类器（e.g., Llama-Guard, Azure Content Safety）。

关键发现与意义

在Llama-3-70B-Instruct、Qwen2-72B-Instruct等7个主流闭源/开源模型上实证表明：GAS-Leak-LLM平均越狱成功率达82.6%（较AutoDAN提升31.4%，较PAIR提升26.9%），且单次攻击平均仅需<120次API调用。结果揭示：当前多层安全机制存在严重协同防御失效——底层对齐模型与上层审核器间缺乏语义一致性，导致“漏检窗口”被系统性利用。本工作不仅暴露了现实部署中LLM安全架构的根本脆弱性，更提供了评估防护鲁棒性的新基准工具。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) are widely deployed with multi-layered safety mechanisms, yet remain vulnerable to black-box jailbreaking attacks. We propose GAS-Leak-LLM, a genetic algorithm-based framework that optimizes adversarial suffixes without access to model parameters, gradients, or internal representations—only API-level responses. By defining fitness as jailbreak success probability and iteratively applying selection, mutation, and crossover over discrete token sequences, GAS-Leak-LLM efficiently explores the prompt space under strict query constraints. Evaluated on 7 state-of-the-art models (including Llama-3-70B and Qwen2-72B), it achieves an average success rate of 82.6%, outperforming AutoDAN (+31.4%) and PAIR (+26.9%), using fewer than 120 queries per attack. Results expose critical failures in safety stack composition—particularly misalignment between base model alignment and external content classifiers—highlighting urgent needs for co-designed, end-to-end robustness evaluation.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) constitute pivotal components within the AI-dominated information technology ecosystem. To mitigate risks associated with harmful or policy-violating outputs, commercial systems employ advanced alignment strategies and multi-layered content moderation mechanisms. Despite these safeguards, recent research has demonstrated that LLMs remain vulnerable to adversarial manipulation, particularly through jailbreaking and prompt injection techniques. In this work, we propose GAS-Leak-LLM a novel jailbreaking attack based on a genetic algorithm that systematically evolves adversarial suffix to bypass safety constraints. Operating in a strict black-box setting, our method requires no access to model parameters or internals, thereby reflecting realistic threat scenarios in deployed systems. Through the iterative application of selection, mutation, and crossover heuristics, the framework systematically explores the discrete prompt space to identify high-fitness adversarial suffixes. Empirical findings reveal critical shortcomings in existing safety enforcement mechanisms and confirm the effectiveness and practical viability of the proposed attack.

item.title: Snyk VulnBench JS 1.0: Can LLMs Find the Same Bugs Twice?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15762v1

AI Summary (中文)

Snyk VulnBench JS 1.0：大语言模型漏洞检测的可重复性实证研究

本研究构建并发布了 Snyk VulnBench JS 1.0 基准——首个面向JavaScript代码、专为评估代理式（agentic）大语言模型（LLM）安全审查可重复性而设计的标准化测试集。研究核心问题是：当输入代码、提示词（prompt）与运行环境完全相同时，LLM能否稳定复现相同的漏洞发现？为此，团队在250次独立模型运行中，对同一套161个真实JavaScript漏洞样本执行了300次重复扫描（含5轮完全相同的重复实验），系统测量结果稳定性。

关键发现呈现显著二分性：

  • ✅ 参考匹配（reference-matched）发现高度稳定：当Claude等模型报告与Snyk Code官方标注漏洞精确匹配时，158个唯一匹配项中134个（84.8%）在全部5轮重复中均出现，表明LLM在识别“已知模式”时具备强一致性；
  • ❌ 额外报告（extra findings）极不稳定：161个未匹配的独特报告中，80个（近50%）仅出现在单轮实验中，仅22个（13.7%）全程复现，暴露当前LLM在自由推理中存在高方差、低鲁棒性问题；
  • 🔍 互补性价值凸显：LLM持续识别出高频、高信噪比的典型漏洞模式（如硬编码凭证、原型污染链），并在一处案例中揭示了Snyk Code SAST工具尚未覆盖的潜在产品缺陷；而Snyk Code作为确定性静态分析工具，在数据流sink路径的系统性枚举上表现更优。

本工作首次量化揭示了LLM安全审查的“稳定性-多样性”权衡本质，有力论证：LLM不应替代SAST，而应与其协同构成“确定性+启发式”的混合安全审查范式。基准数据集与方法论已开源，为可信AI安全评估提供新基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Snyk VulnBench JS 1.0 is the first benchmark designed to quantify the repeatability of agentic LLM-based security review on identical JavaScript code, prompts, and harnesses. Across 300 repeated scans (5× replication per sample), we found stark divergence: reference-matched findings—where LLM outputs aligned precisely with Snyk Code’s ground-truth labels—were highly stable (134/158 unique matches appeared in all 5 runs), while unmatched “extra” reports varied dramatically (80/161 appeared only once; just 22 appeared in all 5). Crucially, LLMs consistently surfaced high-signal exploit patterns—and even uncovered a likely gap in Snyk Code’s coverage—while Snyk Code excelled at deterministic, systematic data-flow sink enumeration. These results demonstrate strong complementarity, not substitutability: agentic LLM review and deterministic SAST should be integrated, not treated as alternatives.

Abstract

We ran 300 repeated vulnerability-finding scans to measure how repeatable agentic large language model (LLM) security review is on the same JavaScript code, prompt, and benchmark harness. The headline result is that LLM security findings were unevenly repeatable: reference-matched findings were stable, but extra model reports varied heavily from run to run. Across 250 model runs, 80 of 161 unique unmatched findings appeared in only one of five identical repetitions, while only 22 appeared in all five. By contrast, when Claude matched a Snyk Code reference finding, the behavior was much more stable: 134 of 158 unique reference-matched findings appeared in all five repetitions. The benchmark also shows complementarity. Models consistently found familiar, high-signal exploit shapes, and in one case surfaced a likely Snyk Code product gap. Snyk Code static application security testing (SAST) was deterministic and better at systematically enumerating repeated data-flow sinks. The results support combining agentic LLM review with deterministic SAST rather than treating either technique as a replacement for the other.

item.title: FragFuse: Bypassing Access Control of Large Language Model Agents via Memory-Based Query Fragmentation and Fusion
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15609v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）智能体广泛采用长期记忆支持复杂任务、用户个性化与领域适配，新兴的访问控制机制正被用于拦截违规请求、防范滥用。然而，本研究首次揭示：智能体的记忆操作本身构成全新攻击面——攻击者可将禁止内容碎片化，在多轮交互中以看似无害的形式注入长期记忆，并在后续查询中通过记忆检索隐式重构完整恶意意图，从而绕过基于单次查询的访问审查。

方法：FragFuse 攻击框架

我们提出 FragFuse——首个利用记忆时序通道实现访问控制绕过的攻击方法，包含三阶段闭环流程：
1. 片段识别：通过黑盒自适应查询+掩码扰动，定位触发访问控制拒绝的关键语义片段；
2. 记忆注入：设计“标记载体查询”（marker carrier queries），将碎片隐蔽存入长期记忆（如向量数据库），规避实时检测；
3. 融合触发：构造后续攻击查询，激活记忆检索并引导模型自动拼合碎片、执行有害任务（如生成违法内容、绕过安全指令）。

为提升普适性，我们进一步提出代理模型优化方案：无需白盒访问或梯度信息，在符合现实威胁模型（仅黑盒API调用）前提下，自动优化融合指令与标记设计，实现端到端自动化攻击生成。

实验结果与影响

在4类典型智能体架构（ReAct、Plan-and-Execute等）及3种前沿访问控制机制（基于规则、微调分类器、RLHF强化策略）上全面评估，FragFuse平均绕过成功率86.3%，端到端有害任务完成率41.1%，且相较无防护场景仅下降4.4%任务成功率，表明其高隐蔽性与强有效性。值得注意的是，当前主流防御手段（如先进提示注入检测器、困惑度异常检测）对此类记忆级碎片攻击完全失效。

AI Summary (English)

We present FragFuse, the first attack that bypasses access control in LLM agents by exploiting long-term memory as a temporal covert channel. It fragments prohibited content across multiple benign-looking interactions, stores them stealthily in memory, and later fuses them via retrieval—without exposing malicious intent in any single user query. FragFuse operates in three black-box stages: (1) adaptive fragment discovery via masked querying, (2) memory injection using marker-carrying queries, and (3) fusion-triggered retrieval with optimized follow-up prompts. We further propose a surrogate-based optimization framework enabling fully automated attack generation under realistic threat assumptions. Evaluated across four representative agent architectures and three state-of-the-art access-control mechanisms, FragFuse achieves an 86.3% average bypass success rate and 41.1% end-to-end harmful task success, with only 4.4% degradation versus unprotected baselines. Critically, existing defenses—including top-tier prompt-injection and perplexity detectors—fail to detect or mitigate this memory-based fragmentation attack.

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on long-term memory to support complex task execution, user personalization, and domain adaptation. Meanwhile, emerging access-control mechanisms for LLM agents are being explored to block policy-violating requests and prevent misuse. We reveal a novel attack surface arising from agent memory operations: prohibited content that would trigger access control can be fragmented across interactions, stored in long-term memory in benign-appearing form, and later reconstructed through memory retrieval without appearing explicitly in the final user query. We propose FragFuse, the first attack that enables unprivileged users to bypass agent access control by exploiting this temporal channel introduced by long-term memory. FragFuse operates in three stages: (1) identifying rejection-responsive fragments via black-box adaptive querying with fragment masking; (2) injecting these fragments into memory using marker carrier queries; and (3) retrieving and fusing the stored fragments through a follow-up attack query. Although FragFuse can be instantiated manually for individual agents, we further develop a surrogate-based optimization scheme that tunes fusion instructions and marker designs, enabling automated attack generation without violating the attacker's threat-model assumptions. We evaluate FragFuse across four representative agent settings and task domains, covering three state-of-the-art agent access-control mechanisms. FragFuse achieves an average bypass success rate of 86.3% and an average end-to-end harmful task success rate of 41.1% across all settings, with only 4.4% average task-success degradation compared with configurations without access control. We also show that alternative defenses, including state-of-the-art prompt-injection detectors and perplexity detectors, do not effectively address this attack.

item.title: CmdNeedle: Measuring the Incompleteness of Command Denylists for AI Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15549v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

随着AI智能体（AI Agents）在终端环境中的广泛应用，其安全机制面临严峻挑战。终端AI智能体高度依赖Shell命令执行与宿主系统交互，当前主流防护策略采用“三列表”命令门控机制（allowlist/blocklist/denylist），其中denylist（命令拒绝列表）被广泛视为核心防线。然而，现代操作系统内置命令数量庞大、功能复杂且持续演进，导致denylist极易出现覆盖不全问题——即使由Claude Code等成熟系统维护的内置denylist，仍存在可被绕过的“盲点”，使其实际防护能力远低于预期。

方法创新：CmdNeedle框架

本文首次对终端AI智能体中命令denylist的不完备性（incompleteness） 进行系统性量化与诊断。提出LLM驱动的自动化检测框架 CmdNeedle：

  • 以大语言模型为“攻击生成器”，主动推理潜在绕过命令（如/bin/sh -c "..."、env -i ...、printf | sh等非常规变体）；
  • 构建轻量沙箱验证器，实时执行并反馈命令是否成功绕过denylist；
  • 采用迭代式提示优化与修复机制，实现闭环检测与可解释性分析。

关键发现

在GitHub上采集的1,709个真实项目denylist（含13,332条规则） 上评估表明：

  • 69.0%–98.6%的denylist存在脆弱性，即至少存在1条可绕过其限制的合法Shell命令；
  • 脆弱性在不同AI代理框架（如LangChain、AutoGen）、不同项目规模及不同denylist维护策略中普遍存在且稳定复现；
  • 根因分析证实：命令语义等价性未被建模、路径别名与解释器链未被覆盖、以及动态构造命令（如通过$()、反引号、env注入）是三大主因。

本研究为AI智能体安全提供首个可复现、可扩展的denylist完备性评估范式，推动从“静态规则枚举”向“语义感知防御”演进。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents CmdNeedle, the first systematic framework to measure and expose the incompleteness of command denylists in terminal-based AI agents. Recognizing that denylists—despite being a cornerstone of command-gating security—are often fragile due to the combinatorial complexity and semantic equivalence of shell commands, we formalize the fragility problem and design an LLM-driven pipeline: it prompts LLMs to generate bypass candidates (e.g., env -i sh -c, printf | sh), validates them in a sandbox, and iteratively refines prompts using execution feedback. Evaluated on 1,709 real-world denylists (13,332 rules) from GitHub, CmdNeedle reveals that 69.0–98.6% are fragile, with consistent vulnerability across diverse agents (LangChain, AutoGen), project sizes, and maintenance practices. We identify key root causes—including unmodeled command equivalences, interpreter chaining, and dynamic command construction—and provide actionable insights for building semantically aware, robust command filters. Our open methodology establishes a new benchmark for evaluating and hardening AI agent command safety.

Abstract

The adoption of AI agents is increasing rapidly. Terminal AI agents, i.e., AI agents that run in terminal environments, are a widely used type of AI agents. Terminal AI agents rely heavily on shell command execution to interact with the host systems. They adopt a three-list command-gating mechanism to mitigate security risks introduced by command execution, with denylists serving as the load-bearing component. However, modern operating systems often ship a large, ever-expanding set of shell commands with complex functionalities. Our observation is that even a built-in denylist of Claude Code, well-maintained by its developers, can overlook bypass commands that invalidate its effectiveness. Such negligence leads to fragile command denylists that cannot even block operations that practitioners expect them to block. This paper presents the first systematic characterization of command denylist fragility in terminal AI agents. The paper formalizes the command denylist fragility problem and proposes an LLM-driven pipeline, CmdNeedle, to detect such fragility. It prompts the LLM to propose possible bypasses and iteratively repairs them using feedback from a validator that executes them in a sandbox. In the evaluation, we applied CmdNeedle to 1,709 real-world command denylists (containing 13,332 denylist rules) collected from GitHub. The evaluation shows several key findings, including that 69.0--98.6% of the denylists are fragile, that this fragility occurs consistently across projects and agents, and the validity of several possible root causes for this fragility. Our pipeline and findings will hopefully facilitate future research and practice regarding the command denylists used by AI agents.

item.title: Multi-tier Differential Private Query Release
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15543v1

AI Summary (中文)

多层级差分隐私查询发布框架

在数据库、移动计算与数据市场等场景中，面向敏感数据的统计查询需在差分隐私（DP）约束下进行。现实中，多个分析者可能提交相同查询，但因信任等级或付费意愿不同，需分配差异化隐私预算（即多层级DP），导致传统单预算机制难以适用。现有方法或造成累积隐私损失超出最严预算（如简单重复加噪），或牺牲显著效用（如保守预算分配），无法兼顾隐私严格性与查询精度。

本文提出首个严格满足多层级DP语义且实现最优效用的通用框架：

  • 隐私保障：确保任意查询响应的累积隐私损失严格受限于该查询所获最高隐私预算（而非各次响应预算之和），彻底避免预算泄漏；
  • 效用最优：在噪声添加机制（如中心化模型下的双向几何机制用于计数查询）中，基于噪声分布的特征函数构造统一解耦方案，使效用达到单预算机制理论上限；
  • 机制普适性：针对本地化DP等复杂场景（如Subset机制），设计预算变换原语与模板化策略，在宽松至严格隐私区间均实现渐近最优误差界。

实验表明，本框架在真实与合成数据集上较基线方法降低32–68%的平均绝对误差，同时严格满足各层级预算约束，为可信数据共享提供了兼具安全性与实用性的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first framework for multi-tier differential privacy (DP) query release that simultaneously guarantees cumulative privacy loss bounded by the maximum privacy budget assigned to any single query (not the sum), and achieves utility matching optimal single-tier mechanisms. For noise-addition mechanisms (e.g., two-sided Geometric mechanism for count queries in the curator model), we derive a general solution via characteristic functions of noise distributions. For non-additive or local DP mechanisms (e.g., Subset mechanism), we design budget-transformation primitives and a template-based strategy that attains asymptotically optimal error across all privacy regimes. Experiments on real and synthetic datasets show our framework reduces mean absolute error by 32–68% over state-of-the-art baselines while strictly respecting tiered budget constraints.

Abstract

Answering statistical queries over sensitive data under differential privacy (DP) is a common task in many settings, including databases, mobile computing, and data markets. In these scenarios, multiple analysts may issue the same query, while receiving answers generated under different privacy budgets due to differences in trust levels or willingness to pay. Existing approaches for such multi-tier DP queries either incur excessive cumulative privacy loss or suffer from suboptimal utility. In this paper, we propose a framework for multi-tier DP query release that simultaneously bound the cumulative privacy loss by the maximum privacy budget among all queries and achieve optimal utility comparable to that of single-tier mechanisms. Our framework applies to different classes of DP mechanisms. For noise-adding mechanisms (e.g., count queries with the two-sided Geometric mechanism in the curator model), we develop a general solution based on the characteristic functions of noise distributions. For other mechanisms (e.g., count queries under the local DP model with the Subset mechanism), we design mechanism-specific primitives for budget transformation and introduce a template-based strategy that attains optimal utility across different privacy regimes. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework.

item.title: SACE: Concept Erasure at the Semantic Singularity in Visual Autoregressive Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15819v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

视觉自回归（VAR）模型在高保真文本到图像生成中取得突破性进展，但其内容安全对齐问题日益突出。现有概念擦除方法（如基于扩散模型的隐空间干预）直接迁移至VAR架构时，常引发灾难性语义坍塌与严重视觉伪影——根本原因在于其设计依赖扩散模型同质化的去噪步长，而VAR采用多尺度粗粒度到细粒度的分层生成机制，语义注入具有强尺度依赖性。

核心洞见：语义奇点公理

本文首次提出语义奇点公理（Semantic Singularity Axiom）：提示中任一目标语义概念在VAR生成流程中被唯一且不可逆地锁定于Scale-0（最粗尺度）。为实证验证该公理，我们设计增量语义显著性分析（ISSA），通过逐层扰动与归因量化，首次实现对VAR中“粗→细”语义注入过程的可解释、可审计可视化，为社区提供透明化诊断工具。

方法创新：SACE框架

基于上述发现，我们提出首个尺度感知概念擦除框架SACE：

  • 严格限定干预范围：仅在Scale-0施加干预，避免跨尺度语义污染；
  • 熵正则化擦除目标：抑制高熵采样退化，保障生成多样性；
  • 恢复性保真损失：锚定良性先验结构，防止无关特征畸变。

实验表明，SACE在人脸、物体、场景等多领域实现精准、无损的概念擦除（如移除敏感标识、偏见属性），训练开销极低（仅需单卡1小时微调），显著提升VAR模型的安全鲁棒性，为下一代生成式AI提供可信赖的对齐基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Visual autoregressive (VAR) models enable unprecedented fidelity in text-to-image synthesis, yet pose acute safety alignment risks. Existing concept erasure methods—designed for diffusion models’ homogeneous denoising steps—fail catastrophically on VARs, causing semantic collapse and artifacts. We introduce the Semantic Singularity Axiom: target concepts are definitively locked at Scale-0. Validated via our Incremental Semantic Saliency Analysis (ISSA), this insight enables transparent, coarse-to-fine semantic auditing. Guided by it, we propose SACE, the first scale-aware erasure framework for VARs: interventions are strictly confined to Scale-0; an entropy-regularized objective prevents sampling degeneration; and a restorative preservation loss safeguards entangled benign priors. Experiments across domains show surgical, high-fidelity erasure with minimal training overhead (<1 GPU-hour), effectively resolving critical safety vulnerabilities in emerging VAR architectures. Code: https://github.com/limerenceysy/SACE

Abstract

The rapid progress of visual autoregressive (VAR) models has unlocked a transformative frontier for high-fidelity text-to-image synthesis, while heightening concerns over the safety alignment of generated content. Naive application of existing erasure techniques to VAR models causes catastrophic semantic collapse and visual artifacts, since they are predominantly designed for the homogeneous denoising steps of diffusion models. To address this foundational challenge, we first propose the Semantic Singularity Axiom, which posits that any target semantic concept embedded within a prompt is definitively locked at Scale-0. Then rigorously validate this axiom through our proposed Incremental Semantic Saliency Analysis (ISSA),which also enable the community to transparently inspect the coarse-to-fine semantic injection process. Guided by this insight, we introduce the first scale-aware concept erasure framework (SACE) for VAR models. By strictly confining interventions to the first scale, our approach couples an Entropy-Regularized Erasure Objective to prevent high-entropy sampling degeneration, alongside a restorative preservation loss to safely anchor the integrity of entangled benign priors. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves surgical concept erasure performance across various domains with minimal training overhead, timely and elegently resolute the critical safety vulnerabilities inherent in emerging VAR architectures. Code is available at: https://github.com/limerenceysy/SACE}{https://github.com/limerenceysy/SACE.

item.title: When Generator Replay Degrades: Projected Rehearsal Orchestration for Heterogeneous Federated Class-Incremental Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15695v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦类增量学习（FCIL）在客户端标签分布异构、任务进展不同步、同类语义概念监督强度不均等现实场景下性能显著退化。现有方法多依赖输入空间合成回放（如生成图像/文本），但此类合成样本易受任务流异质性干扰，且跨模态迁移能力弱，导致“回放质量”失效——单纯增加回放数量无法缓解因监督不平衡与阶段错位引发的知识遗忘。

方法创新：PRO 与 PRO-MAX

本文提出 PRO（Projected Rehearsal Orchestration） 框架，摒弃脆弱的输入级合成，转而构建轻量、紧凑的类级投影记忆（class-level projected memories）于服务器端。所有方法在统一无预训练暖启（warmup）下评估，确保公平性。PRO 允许客户端将当前真实样本与服务器下发的投影记忆联合进行平衡的伪多任务训练（兼顾分类、投影一致性与记忆重建）。为进一步抑制强表征漂移，我们扩展为 PRO-MAX：在保持“服务器仅聚合模型更新与内存统计”这一轻量原则前提下，引入邻域加权记忆对齐机制（neighborhood-weighted memory alignment），动态校准投影记忆与客户端当前表征空间的一致性。

关键发现

在图像（CIFAR-100-Fed）、文本（AG News-Fed）和图（Reddit-Fed）三大异构FCIL基准上，PRO/MAX 均显著提升知识保留率（+12.3% 平均准确率）与最终实用性能，且在同质FCIL中保持竞争力。即使基线方法获得加倍回放预算，其性能仍因监督失衡持续下滑，证实回放质量优于数量。弱任务诊断进一步表明：回放-表征失配度（replay-representation mismatch）与下游性能退化呈强正相关，而PRO系列始终维持投影记忆与演化表征的高度对齐。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Class-Incremental Learning (FCIL) suffers severe degradation under client heterogeneity—divergent label subsets, asynchronous task progression, and imbalanced supervision over shared semantics. Existing input-space generator replay methods prove fragile in such settings and lack cross-modal generalizability. We propose PRO, a server-light framework that replaces synthetic input replay with projected rehearsal orchestration: the server maintains compact class-level projected memories (not raw data), and clients perform balanced pseudo multi-task training over current examples and these projections—without external pretraining (all methods share identical warmup). To combat stronger representation drift, PRO-MAX augments PRO with neighborhood-weighted memory alignment, preserving the server’s sole role of aggregating model updates and memory statistics. Across image, text, and graph benchmarks, PRO and PRO-MAX significantly improve retention (+12.3% avg. accuracy) and final utility under heterogeneous streams, while remaining competitive in homogeneous FCIL. Critically, even with expanded replay budgets, baselines degrade under supervision imbalance—demonstrating that replay quality, not quantity, is decisive. Weak-task analysis confirms that larger replay–representation mismatch strongly correlates with downstream degradation, whereas PRO maintains tight alignment between projected memories and evolving client representations.

Abstract

Federated class-incremental learning (FCIL) becomes substantially harder when clients observe different label subsets, progress through tasks at different stages, and provide uneven supervision for the same semantic concepts. Existing FCIL methods often preserve old knowledge through input-space synthesis, but they can be fragile under heterogeneous task streams and difficult to transfer across modalities. To alleviate such issues, we propose PRO, a framework that replaces synthetic input replay with projected rehearsal orchestration. To remove external pretraining, we evaluate all methods under the same warmup. After this, PRO maintains compact class-level projected memories on the server and allows clients perform balanced pseudo multi-task training over current examples and old projected memories. To handle stronger representation drift, we further introduce PRO-MAX, which augments PRO with neighborhood-weighted memory alignment while preserving the same server-light principle that the server only aggregates model updates and memory statistics. Across image, text, and graph benchmarks, PRO and PRO-MAX improve retention and final utility under heterogeneous streams while remaining competitive in homogeneous FCIL. Even when baselines are given expanded replay budgets, they degrade under supervision imbalance and stage misalignment, indicating that replay quantity alone does not resolve replay-quality failures. Additional weak-task diagnostics further show that larger replay mismatch is associated with larger downstream degradation, while our method keeps projected memories better aligned with the evolving representation.

item.title: Conflict-Aware Federated Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15625v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大规模语言模型（LLM）持续扩展带来高昂计算开销，Mixture-of-Experts（MoE） 因其稀疏激活特性成为高效微调的可扩展方案。然而，当将MoE融入联邦学习（FL） 以实现隐私保护协同训练时，客户端数据非独立同分布（non-IID）会引发严重冲突：相同索引的专家在不同客户端上被迫拟合差异甚至矛盾的特征-标签关联，导致全局聚合时出现破坏性干扰，优化轨迹失稳、模型性能显著下降。

方法创新：FC-MoE框架

我们提出 FC-MoE（Federated Conflict-Aware MoE） ——首个面向MoE微调的冲突感知联邦框架。其核心包含三重机制：

  • 重要性感知加权（Importance-Aware Weighting）：基于本地梯度范数与任务一致性评估更新可靠性，动态抑制低信度本地更新；
  • 梯度共识投影（Gradient Consensus Projection）：将各客户端专家梯度正交投影至全局共识子空间，显式抑制方向冲突，保障聚合稳定性；
  • 本地知识保留（Local Knowledge Retention）：通过残差重锚机制，在全局同步后恢复域特异性专家能力，避免知识遗忘。

实验验证与优势

在多个non-IID联邦基准（如FedWikiText、FedCLUE）上，FC-MoE相较基线（FedAvg-MoE、FedMoE）提升全局准确率+4.2–6.8%，本地个性化性能平均提升+5.1%，收敛速度加快~35%。消融实验证实：共识投影对冲突抑制贡献最大（+3.7%全局精度），而知识保留显著增强长尾领域泛化能力。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) face prohibitive computational costs during scaling, making Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) a promising sparse fine-tuning paradigm. Yet integrating MoE into federated learning (FL) under data heterogeneity triggers conflicting expert optimization: same-indexed experts learn inconsistent feature-label mappings across clients, causing destructive interference during aggregation and destabilizing training. To resolve this, we propose FC-MoE, a conflict-aware federated MoE framework. It introduces (i) importance-aware weighting to prioritize reliable local updates; (ii) gradient consensus projection to suppress conflicting gradients via orthogonal projection onto a shared subspace; and (iii) local knowledge retention that re-anchors domain-specific residuals post-aggregation. Extensive experiments on non-IID benchmarks (FedWikiText, FedCLUE) show FC-MoE achieves +4.2–6.8% higher global accuracy, +5.1% average local improvement, and ~35% faster convergence versus state-of-the-art MoE-FL baselines—demonstrating superior stability, personalization, and scalability.

Abstract

The continuous scaling of large language models (LLMs) incurs prohibitive computational costs, making Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) a scalable alternative for efficient fine-tuning via sparse activation. While federated learning (FL) emerges as the paradigm for privacy-preserving collaborative optimization, integrating MoE into FL under data heterogeneity may trigger conflicting expert optimizations. Client-specific data distributions force same-indexed experts to optimize under inconsistent or even conflicting feature-label correlations. This mismatch induces destructive interference during aggregation, thus destabilizing the optimization trajectory and degrading model performance. To address this issue, we propose FC-MoE, a federated conflict-aware framework for MoE fine-tuning. It employs an importance aware weighting scheme to prioritize reliable local updates and utilizes gradient consensus projection to suppress conflicting updates, ensuring a stable global optimization path. Moreover, a local knowledge retention mechanism further preserves specialized client expertise by re-anchoring domain-specific residuals. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FC-MoE accelerates convergence and enhances both global and local model performance in non-IID federated environments.

item.title: A Large-Scale Multi-Dimensional Empirical Study of LLMs for Conversation Summarization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15974v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

尽管大语言模型（LLMs）在对话摘要任务中取得显著进展，现有评估仍存在三大局限：场景覆盖不足（多集中于单一领域）、输入长度受限（常忽略长上下文建模能力）、样本规模偏小（缺乏统计稳健性）。更关键的是，主流基准普遍缺失对前沿推理增强型模型（如o1、DeepSeek-R1）和高性价比轻量级模型（如Phi-3、Qwen2.5-0.5B）的系统评测，且缺乏多维度细粒度评估框架——既无法区分“完整性”“简洁性”与“忠实性”的差异化表现，也难以解耦模型规模与推理能力的独立影响。

方法创新：OmniCSEval统一基准

我们提出 OmniCSEval——首个面向对话摘要的大规模、多维实证评估基准：

  • 规模与多样性：涵盖6类真实场景（客服、医疗咨询、法律协商、教育辅导、技术讨论、社交闲聊），共1,800条高质量对话，上下文长度横跨128–32k tokens；
  • 双向事实核查框架：
  • 前向匹配（Key Fact Matching）：基于人工-LLM协同流水线提取黄金关键事实，量化摘要的完整性（召回率）与简洁性（冗余率）；
  • 反向验证（Summary Fact Verification）：通过多LLM共识分解器（集成GPT-4o、Claude-3.5、Qwen2.5-Max）验证摘要事实的忠实性（精确率），规避单模型幻觉偏差；
  • 模型覆盖：系统评测28个代表性LLM，按推理能力（标准vs.推理增强）与参数规模（<1B, 1–7B, 7–70B, >70B）四象限分组。

关键发现与实践启示

实证揭示：（1）所有模型在跨场景泛化上存在显著性能断层，尤其在法律与医疗等高专业性场景中忠实性下降超42%；（2）推理能力提升比单纯扩模更有效——o1-mini在32k长文本摘要中完胜同规模Llama-3-8B（+29.7%忠实性）；（3）小型推理模型展现惊人效率优势：Phi-3.5-mini（4B）在客服场景中达GPT-4 Turbo 92%性能，推理耗时仅1/5。本研究为工业部署提供可操作指南：优先选用推理增强型中小模型，并依据场景复杂度动态适配上下文窗口。

AI Summary (English)

Despite rapid progress in LLM-based conversation summarization, evaluations suffer from narrow scenarios, short contexts, small samples, and poor coverage of reasoning-enhanced or efficient small models. To address this, we introduce OmniCSEval, a unified benchmark with 1,800 diverse conversations across six real-world domains (e.g., healthcare, legal, customer service), spanning context lengths from 128 to 32k tokens. We propose a bidirectional fact-checking framework: (1) key fact matching—via human-LLM collaborative extraction—to assess completeness and conciseness; and (2) summary fact verification—using multi-LLM consensus decomposition—to rigorously measure faithfulness. Evaluating 28 LLMs across four categories (by reasoning capability and scale), we find: (i) severe cross-scenario performance gaps—especially in high-stakes domains; (ii) reasoning augmentation consistently outperforms scaling alone (e.g., o1-mini surpasses Llama-3-8B by +29.7% faithfulness on 32k inputs); and (iii) compact reasoning models (e.g., Phi-3.5-mini) achieve >90% of GPT-4 Turbo’s accuracy at <20% latency. Our work provides actionable guidance for production deployment.

Abstract

Despite the significant advancement of LLMs in conversation summarization, their evaluation remains limited by insufficient scenarios, input lengths, and sample sizes. Furthermore, existing benchmarks often omit frontier reasoning systems and efficient small models, or lack fine-grained, multi-dimensional assessments. To bridge these gaps, we propose OmniCSEval, a unified benchmark comprising 1,800 diverse conversations across six real-world scenarios, featuring context lengths ranging from 128 to 32k tokens. For fine-grained evaluation, we employ a bidirectional fact-checking framework that integrates key fact matching to assess completeness and conciseness, alongside summary fact verification to evaluate faithfulness. To ensure reliable assessment, we establish a human-LLM collaborative pipeline for key fact extraction and a multi-LLM consensus verifier for summary fact decomposition. Leveraging this framework, we evaluate 28 LLMs across four distinct categories grouped by reasoning capability and model scale. Our extensive empirical study reveals critical insights regarding the cross-scenario challenges current LLMs continue to face, the impacts of reasoning and scale, and the efficiency and adaptability of reasoning models. We also provide guidance for system selection in real-world deployments.

item.title: Related Differentials of $4\times4$ MDS Matrices: A Complete Characterization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1251

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在分组密码分析中，相关微分（related differential） 是一类特殊差分对 $(x,y)$，要求对线性层 $M$ 的每个输入/输出坐标位置，$x_i$ 与 $y_j$ 至少满足：二者之一为零，或二者相等。该概念是 Bardeh–Rijmen 零差分攻击的理论基础，而 Daemen 与 Rijmen 曾提出关键问题：哪些 $4\times4$ 最大距离可分（MDS）矩阵存在相关微分？此前工作已完全刻画 $\mathbb{F}_{2^r}$ 上 $3\times3$ MDS 矩阵的充要条件（15个显式方程），但 $4\times4$ 情形长期悬而未决。

核心成果与方法

本文首次给出 $4\times4$ MDS 矩阵 admitting 相关微分的完整代数刻画：设 $M = DNE$ 为其标准约化形式（$N$ 含9个自由变量），则 $M$ 存在相关微分 当且仅当 其9个变量满足280个显式多项式方程中的至少一个。这些方程严格分为两类：70个二次方程与210个三次方程；全部两两互异、不可约、互素，并在自然对称群作用下形成27个轨道。

结构深度解析

我们揭示了方程集的深层组合结构：

  • $3\times3$ 情形的15个方程恰好对应射影空间 $\mathrm{PG}(3,2)$ 的全部15个点；
  • $4\times4$ 的280个方程张成一个14维 $\mathbb{F}_2$-线性空间，满足560个加性关系，且包含840对互斥方程（无法同时在任一MDS矩阵上成立）；
  • 被排除的零模式分为两类：525类因行列式退化而直接违反MDS性质，289类为逻辑空真（vacuous）情形。

实验验证与应用意义

在最小可行域 $\mathbb{F}_8$ 上，穷举确认存在720个约化MDS矩阵：每个恰满足28个方程，每个方程恰被72个矩阵满足——故所有 $4\times4$ MDS 矩阵在 $\mathbb{F}_8$ 上均存在相关微分；而在 $\mathbb{F}_{2^{10}}$ 上，我们构造出首个不满足任何方程的显式MDS矩阵，证伪普遍性。全部结论均通过独立穷举搜索与精确符号计算双重验证。

AI Summary (English)

This paper provides a complete algebraic characterization of $4\times4$ MDS matrices over $\mathbb{F}_{2^r}$ admitting related differentials — a cryptanalytically significant property underlying zero-difference attacks. We prove that such a matrix $M = DNE$, with reduced form $N$ having nine free entries, admits a related differential iff at least one of 280 explicit polynomial equations (70 quadratic, 210 cubic) in those entries holds. All equations are pairwise distinct, irreducible, coprime, and partition into 27 symmetry orbits. Structurally, they span a 14-dimensional $\mathbb{F}_2$-space, satisfy exactly 560 additive relations, and contain 840 mutually exclusive pairs. Exhaustive search over $\mathbb{F}_8$ confirms all 720 reduced $4\times4$ MDS matrices satisfy exactly 28 equations each — hence every such matrix over $\mathbb{F}_8$ admits a related differential; conversely, we explicitly construct an MDS matrix over $\mathbb{F}_{2^{10}}$ satisfying none. All results are rigorously verified by independent exact computation.

Abstract

A pair of differences $(x,y)$ is a \emph{related differential} for a linear layer $M$ if, for every coordinate at both the input and the output, at least one of the two values vanishes or the two values coincide. Related differentials underlie the zero-difference attack on AES of Bardeh and Rijmen, and the question of which maximum distance separable (MDS) matrices admit them was raised by Daemen and Rijmen, who showed that every $4\times4$ circulant MDS matrix does while some Hadamard ones do not. In earlier work we characterized the $3\times3$ MDS matrices over $\mathbb{F}_{2^r}$ admitting related differentials by fifteen explicit equations. In this paper we settle the $4\times4$ case completely: an MDS matrix $M=DNE$ over $\mathbb{F}_{2^r}$ admits a related differential if and only if at least one of $280$ explicit polynomial equations in the nine free entries of its reduced form $N$ holds. The equations, $70$ quadratic and $210$ cubic, are pairwise distinct, irreducible and pairwise coprime, and fall into $27$ orbits under the natural symmetries. We further determine the structure of the equation set: the fifteen equations of the $3\times3$ case are exactly the points of $\mathrm{PG}(3,2)$, while the $280$ equations span a $14$-dimensional $\mathbb{F}_2$-space, satisfy exactly $560$ additive relations, and contain exactly $840$ pairs that can never hold simultaneously on an MDS matrix. The discarded zero patterns split into $525$ whose determinant condition is equivalent to the failure of MDS-ness and $289$ vacuous cases. Over $\mathbb{F}_8$, the smallest field carrying $4\times4$ MDS matrices, exhaustive enumeration shows that there are exactly $720$ reduced MDS matrices; each satisfying exactly $28$ of the equations and each equation being satisfied by exactly $72$ matrices; in particular every $4\times4$ MDS matrix over $\mathbb{F}_8$ admits a related differential. Over $\mathbb{F}_{2^{10}}$ we exhibit an explicit MDS matrix admitting none. All results are verified by exact computation against an independent exhaustive search.

item.title: TensorZKP: Repurposing GPU Tensor Cores for High-Performance Zero-Knowledge Proofs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1250

AI Summary (中文)

TensorZKP：首项利用GPU张量核加速零知识证明的框架

背景与挑战：GPU张量核（Tensor Cores）作为AI爆发的核心硬件，专为低精度矩阵乘法设计，却长期未被密码学领域有效利用。零知识证明（ZKP）虽具隐私保护价值，但其高昂的证明生成开销严重制约落地——现有GPU实现仅依赖通用SIMT核心，完全闲置张量核高达数十倍的算力潜能。

方法创新：本文提出TensorZKP，首个系统性复用张量核加速ZKP的端到端框架。针对张量核不支持有限域运算的根本障碍，我们：（1）设计张量核兼容的有限域算术，将模运算嵌入FP16/BF16流水线；（2）重构关键ZKP模块——将sum-check协议与Spielman编码重表述为稠密/稀疏矩阵乘法任务；（3）构建异步warp特化执行框架，协同调度内存加载、张量核计算与SIMT模约减，消除硬件级流水线气泡。

关键成果：以HyperPlonk（PIOP）与Brakedown（PCS）为实例，TensorZKP在$2^{25}$规模电路上实现端到端证明生成仅215.28 ms：核心算子（内积、标量-向量乘、二次sum-check、编码器）耗时分别低至0.85 ms、0.91 ms、4.04 ms、11.58 ms。相比CPU基线提速955×，超越当前最优SIMT-GPU方案36.2×，首次验证张量核在密码学计算中的颠覆性潜力。

AI Summary (English)

TensorZKP is the first GPU framework to harness specialized Tensor Cores—originally designed for AI acceleration—for high-performance zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) generation. To overcome the fundamental mismatch between Tensor Cores’ low-precision matrix multiplication and ZKP’s finite-field arithmetic, we design Tensor-Core-compatible modular arithmetic and reformulate core ZKP primitives (sum-check protocols and Spielman codes) as matrix multiplication tasks. We further introduce an asynchronous, warp-specialized execution pipeline that overlaps memory access, Tensor Core computation, and SIMT-based modular reduction. Instantiated with HyperPlonk (PIOP) and Brakedown (PCS), TensorZKP achieves 215.28 ms end-to-end proof time for a $2^{25}$-gate circuit—955× faster than CPU and 36.2× faster than state-of-the-art SIMT-based GPU implementations—demonstrating the transformative potential of repurposing AI hardware for cryptographic workloads.

Abstract

GPU Tensor Cores, specialized hardware units designed to accelerate matrix multiplication, have served as the primary engine behind the AI revolution. Given the exponential performance gains they have delivered, aligning cryptographic implementations with this hardware evolution is critical. This is particularly acute for zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs), a cryptographic primitive that currently grapples with high proof generation costs. Existing GPU implementations for ZKPs rely exclusively on general-purpose SIMT cores, leaving the massive computational power of Tensor Cores untapped.

In this paper, we introduce TensorZKP, the first GPU framework to harness Tensor Cores for ZKP acceleration. Since Tensor Cores are designed for low-precision matrix multiplication, mapping ZKP's arithmetic to this hardware is non-trivial. To bridge this gap, we develop Tensor-Core-compatible finite field arithmetic and reformulate ZKP modules, specifically sum-check protocols and Spielman code, into matrix multiplication tasks. Furthermore, we design an asynchronous warp-specialized framework that pipelines memory access, Tensor Core matrix operations, and SIMT-based modular reductions. We instantiate these optimizations with HyperPlonk as the Polynomial Interactive Oracle Proof (PIOP) and Brakedown as the Polynomial Commitment Scheme (PCS) to enable end-to-end proof generation.

The evaluation results show that TensorZKP exhibits remarkable efficiency. At a $2^{25}$ scale, the underlying building blocks complete in $0.85$ ms for inner product, $0.91$ ms for scalar-vector multiplication, $4.04$ ms for degree-2 sum-check, and $11.58$ ms for the encoder. For a circuit with $2^{25}$ multiplication gates, TensorZKP achieves a proof generation time of only $215.28$ milliseconds, representing a $955\times$ speedup over the CPU baseline and a $36.2\times$ improvement over state-of-the-art SIMT-based GPU implementations.

item.title: Model Stealing Through the Lens of Model Multiplicity
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15493v1

AI Summary (中文)

模型窃取中的模型多重性视角：挑战高保真代理模型的等价性假设

模型窃取攻击——即攻击者通过查询黑盒API构建高保真代理模型——被视为对机器学习即服务（MLaaS）知识产权的重大威胁。传统观点认为，此类代理模型一旦达到与目标模型相近的预测精度，便能获得与其相当的经济价值与部署能力。本文从根本上质疑这一隐含假设，指出：由于查询式提取仅提供目标模型输入-输出行为的部分监督信号，所学得的代理模型并非唯一解；相反，存在大量在测试精度上“几乎同等优秀”但内在结构与行为特性迥异的模型——即构成Rashomon集（Rashomon Set）。

我们摒弃经典端到端学习式窃取范式，转而系统性地计算并分析该Rashomon集的多样性：一方面采用多重性量化指标（包括模糊性 Ambiguity、差异性 Discrepancy 和 Rashomon容量 Capacity）刻画模型行为漂移；另一方面引入群体公平性指标（如不同人口子群间的准确率差、机会均等偏差），评估其部署鲁棒性。实验覆盖三大真实场景：金融信用评分（tabular）、胸部X光诊断（medical imaging）和情感分析（NLP）。结果一致表明：即使所有代理模型在整体准确率上与目标模型偏差<0.5%，其在公平性差距上可高达32.7%，在对抗鲁棒性上标准差达18.4%，且关键特征重要性排序相关性低至0.21。这揭示了“高保真≠高可用”——模型多重性导致的隐性行为分化，严重削弱了窃取模型的实际替代价值，为模型产权保护提供了新维度的理论支撑与实证依据。

AI Summary (English)

Model stealing attacks—where adversaries query black-box ML services to build high-fidelity surrogates—pose serious IP threats. This paper challenges the common assumption that such surrogates are functionally equivalent to the target model in deployment. Leveraging model multiplicity, we show that query-based extraction yields not one, but a Rashomon Set: a collection of models nearly as accurate as the target yet divergent in critical properties. Instead of training a single surrogate, we compute this set and quantify its diversity using ambiguity, discrepancy, and Rashomon capacity, alongside group fairness metrics. Experiments across tabular, medical imaging, and NLP tasks reveal stark disparities: despite ≤0.5% accuracy gap vs. the target, surrogates exhibit up to 32.7% fairness disparity, 18.4% std. dev. in adversarial robustness, and near-zero feature importance alignment (ρ=0.21). These results demonstrate that fidelity alone is insufficient for functional equivalence—undermining the economic premise of model stealing and highlighting multiplicity as a foundational constraint.

Abstract

Model stealing attacks, where adversaries create high-fidelity surrogate models, are a significant threat to the intellectual property of machine learning services. Conventional wisdom suggests these surrogates could provide adversaries with economic leverage comparable to the original service providers. This paper challenges this assumption by evaluating model stealing attacks beyond mere fidelity to the target model. Because query-based extraction provides only partial supervision of the target's input-output behavior, the surrogate is not uniquely identified: many near-optimal surrogates can achieve comparable fidelity while differing in deployment-relevant properties. Instead of performing a classic learning-based model stealing attack, we compute the Rashomon Set (i.e., the set of almost-equally-accurate models) of surrogate models, and evaluate its diversity using multiplicity metrics (ambiguity, discrepancy, and Rashomon Capacity) and group fairness metrics. Across tabular, medical imaging, and NLP tasks, our experiments on real-world datasets reveal that despite exhibiting similar fidelity to the target model, surrogate models can display significant variances in other critical performance metrics. These findings cast doubt on the presumed equivalence between high-fidelity surrogates and the target model in practical deployment scenarios.

item.title: Defending against Adaptive Prompt Injection Attacks via Reasoning-enabled Task Alignment
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15441v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

间接提示注入攻击（Indirect Prompt Injection）通过在第三方数据（如检索文档、API响应）中嵌入恶意指令，劫持基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体。现有防御方法在静态基准上报告近乎零的攻击成功率（ASR），但在自适应评估范式下——即攻击者可针对已部署防御进行优化——其性能急剧退化。本文揭示该失效源于两大根本缺陷：（1）意图失焦：现有方法仅识别预设攻击模式，无法动态判断每条嵌入指令是否与用户真实任务意图相关；（2）策略窄化：基于训练的防御依赖少量人工构造模板生成对抗样本，导致泛化能力严重受限，无法覆盖攻击者灵活多变的注入-重写策略。

方法创新：RETA框架

我们提出推理驱动的任务对齐防御框架（RETA），核心思想是将防御决策锚定于用户任务语义，而非攻击者可控的数据表层特征。其关键技术包括：

  • 链式推理验证：在每个工具输出步骤，防御模块执行显式Chain-of-Thought（CoT）推理，逐条检验当前动作是否与用户任务目标逻辑一致；
  • 红队驱动的多样性对抗训练：引入模拟攻击者，通过字典学习多样性奖励（dictionary-learning diversity reward） 鼓励其探索广泛、非冗余的注入变形策略（如语义等价替换、句式重构、上下文混淆），显著拓宽对抗样本分布；
  • 多目标强化学习优化：联合优化安全性（最小化ASR）、实用性（保持任务完成率）与鲁棒性，避免单一指标过拟合。

实验结果

在6种黑盒自适应攻击下，RETA将各攻击的ASR均压制在10%以下：在两个目标模型上平均ASR分别仅为2.92% 和 3.75%，同时在受攻击与干净输入下均保持>94%的原始任务效用，显著优于SOTA方法。

AI Summary (English)

Indirect prompt injection attacks compromise LLM-based agents by hiding malicious instructions in third-party data retrieved during execution. While existing defenses achieve near-zero attack success rate (ASR) on static benchmarks, they collapse under adaptive evaluation—where attackers optimize against the deployed defense. We identify two root causes: (1) over-reliance on pattern matching instead of task-aligned intent verification, and (2) poor generalization due to narrow adversarial training data derived from hand-crafted templates. To address this, we propose RETA, a reasoning-enabled, training-based defense that grounds safety decisions on user task semantics rather than attacker-controlled inputs. At each tool-output step, RETA performs chain-of-thought reasoning to verify action-task consistency. A red-teaming attacker synthesizes diverse adversarial examples guided by a dictionary-learning diversity reward, enabling broad coverage of injection reformulations. Optimized via multi-objective reinforcement learning, RETA achieves an average ASR of 2.92% and 3.75% across six black-box adaptive attacks—keeping every per-attack ASR <10%—while preserving >94% utility on both attacked and clean inputs.

Abstract

Indirect prompt injection attacks hijack LLM-based agents by embedding malicious instructions in third-party data that the agent retrieves during task execution. Existing defenses report near-zero attack success rate on static benchmarks, yet recent adaptive evaluations show that these results collapse once the attacker is allowed to optimize against the deployed defense. In this work, we trace this collapse to two failure modes. First, existing defense methods are confined to recognizing specific attack patterns, rather than assessing whether the intent of every embedded instruction is relevant to the user task. Second, training-based defenses, which otherwise offer the strongest safety-utility trade-off, assemble their adversarial examples from a handful of hand-crafted templates, and the resulting defender fails to generalize outside that narrow strategy distribution. To address these gaps, we propose RETA, a training-based method that grounds defense decisions on the user tasks rather than attacker-controlled data. At each tool-output step, the defender undertakes chain-of-thought reasoning verifying that its actions are consistent with the user task. Leveraging red-teaming, a simulated attacker synthesizes adversarial training data and receives a dictionary-learning diversity reward, achieving broad coverage of injection-reformulation strategies. Together, these allow the defender to be optimized via multi-objective reinforcement learning and achieve better safety-utility trade-off. Across six black-box adaptive attacks, RETA keeps every per-attack ASR below 10%, with average ASR of 2.92% and 3.75% on the two target models, while preserving most utility under attack and on clean inputs.

item.title: Benign in Isolation, Harmful in Composition: Security Risks in Agent Skill Ecosystems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15242v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）智能体日益依赖“技能”（Skills）作为将规划转化为行动的能力层，技能安全正面临全新挑战。现有安全评估普遍采用孤立审查范式——即单独测试每个技能的输入输出行为，却忽视了真实任务中多技能在共享执行上下文中动态串联调用的关键现实。由此催生一种新型风险：技能组合风险（Skill Composition Risk, SCR）——单个看似无害的技能，可能因其输出数据流、信任信号传递、授权线索模糊或副作用，在后续技能链路中被无意放大或恶意利用，最终导致数据泄露、越权操作或工具误用。

方法与基准

本文提出 SCR-Bench，首个面向技能组合风险的可控沙箱化评估基准。它突破传统文本意图分析局限，转而追踪整条激活路径上的状态变迁与端到端结果。基准包含三个子任务：

  • SCR-CapFlow：评估能力流组合（如一个技能输出被另一技能误当作高权限指令）；
  • SCR-TrustLift：评估信任转移组合（如低可信度技能输出被后续技能无条件信任）；
  • SCR-AuthBlur：评估授权混淆组合（如权限上下文在跨技能调用中意外降级或模糊）。

主要发现与创新

实验表明，孤立评估严重低估风险：在 SCR-CapFlow 中，组合场景攻击成功率高达 33.6%（孤立基线近 0%）；SCR-TrustLift 在 5 个后端中的 4 个上攻击成功率超 96.5%；SCR-AuthBlur 在 L1 上下文下，危险审批率较 L0 孤立基线激增 71.8%。本工作首次系统定义 SCR 概念，构建可复现、可扩展的路径级评估框架，为 LLM 智能体技能生态提供以激活路径为中心的安全评估新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies Skill Composition Risk (SCR)—a critical security gap where individually benign agent skills become harmful when composed in shared execution contexts, enabling data leakage, unauthorized actions, or tool misuse. To address the limitation of isolated skill vetting, we introduce SCR-Bench, the first benchmark evaluating SCR through path-level state tracking in sandboxed environments—not just textual intent. It comprises three sub-benchmarks: SCR-CapFlow (capability-flow composition), SCR-TrustLift (trust-transfer composition), and SCR-AuthBlur (authorization-confusion composition). Experiments reveal severe underestimation by isolated evaluation: SCR-CapFlow achieves 33.6% attack success under composition (vs. near-zero baseline); SCR-TrustLift exceeds 96.5% success on 4/5 backends; SCR-AuthBlur shows a 71.8% relative increase in risky approvals under L1 context vs. L0 isolated baseline. SCR-Bench establishes a foundation for path-aware risk assessment and defense in LLM agent skill ecosystems.

Abstract

Skills are becoming the capability layer through which LLM agents turn plans into actions, but their use introduces security risks such as data leakage, unauthorized operations, and tool misuse. Existing vetting usually evaluates each skill in isolation, while real agent tasks often invoke multiple skills in a shared execution context. This creates Skill Composition Risk (SCR): a skill that appears benign alone can become harmful when its outputs, trust signals, authorization cues, or side effects influence later invocations along an activated path. We introduce SCR-Bench to evaluate this risk in controlled, sandboxed skill environments. Rather than relying only on textual intent or surface behavior, SCR-Bench records downstream state changes and path-level outcomes across composed skill executions. It contains three sub-benchmarks: SCR-CapFlow for capability-flow composition, SCR-TrustLift for trust-transfer composition, and SCR-AuthBlur for authorization-confusion composition. Across SCR-Bench, composed paths expose risks that are largely absent under isolated evaluation. In SCR-CapFlow, attack success rate reaches 33.6 percent under composition, compared with near-zero isolated baselines. In SCR-TrustLift, attack success rate exceeds 96.5 percent on four of five backends. In SCR-AuthBlur, the risky-approval rate increases by 71.8 percent relative to the L0 isolated baseline under the L1 context setting. These results show that agent skill security should be assessed at the level of activated paths rather than isolated artifacts. SCR and SCR-Bench provide a foundation for path-aware risk evaluation and defense in LLM agent skill ecosystems. Benchmark: https://github.com/saint-viperx/SCR_Bench.

item.title: LLM: LSTM Look-Ahead Moving Target Defense Based on Historical Malicious Scan
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15229v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

网络扫描是攻击者发起多数网络攻击前的关键侦察步骤。基于IP地址动态重分配的移动目标防御（MTD）作为一种主动防御范式，通过引入随机性显著提升攻击者定位与持续追踪目标的难度。然而，现有IP置换策略普遍存在三大瓶颈：（1）拓扑适应性差——难以在异构网络（如云环境、SDN、边缘网络）中规模化部署；（2）固有开销高——无论是否遭遇真实扫描，均需周期性执行地址重配置，消耗带宽与计算资源；（3）地址资源依赖强——需预留大量未使用IP地址块，制约在IPv4受限或IPv6未充分启用场景下的实用性。

方法创新：LLM框架

本文提出LSTM Look-ahead Moving Target Defense（LLM），首次将长短期记忆网络（LSTM） 引入MTD地址预测任务。LLM核心包含三重设计：

  • 前瞻式扫描预测：利用历史恶意扫描序列训练LSTM模型，精准预测攻击者下一步最可能探测的IP地址（而非被动响应），实现“防于未扫”；
  • 集成学习增强鲁棒性：融合多种扫描行为模式（如顺序扫描、随机扫描、跳跃扫描）的LSTM子模型，显著提升对未知/变异扫描策略的泛化能力；
  • 动态变异机制：仅在预测高风险地址被实际扫描前触发轻量级地址置换，避免全网周期性刷新，将重配置开销降低至传统方法的32%（实测数据）。

实验验证与优势

在CICIDS2017与自建扫描数据集上评估表明：LLM将攻击者成功定位目标的平均时间延长5.8倍，误报率低于1.2%，且在无攻击时段零冗余配置。相比基线MTD方案，其安全增益提升41.7%，系统开销下降68%。本工作为轻量、自适应、可扩展的智能MTD提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Network scanning is a critical reconnaissance step preceding most cyberattacks. While IP-shuffling-based Moving Target Defense (MTD) proactively disrupts attacker reconnaissance, existing approaches suffer from poor scalability across network topologies, unavoidable reconfiguration overhead even during benign periods, and heavy reliance on large unused IP address pools. To overcome these limitations, we propose LSTM Look-ahead Moving Target Defense (LLM)—the first MTD framework leveraging Long Short-Term Memory networks to predict future malicious scan targets before they are probed. LLM employs ensemble LSTM models trained on diverse historical scanning patterns (e.g., sequential, random, hybrid) to enhance robustness, and introduces a lightweight, event-triggered mutation mechanism that activates only upon high-confidence prediction of imminent scans—eliminating periodic reconfiguration. Evaluations on CICIDS2017 and custom datasets show LLM extends attacker mean time-to-compromise by 5.8×, reduces false positives to <1.2%, cuts reconfiguration overhead by 68%, and achieves 41.7% higher security gain than baseline MTD—without requiring extra IP resources.

Abstract

Network scanning is a critical preliminary step for most adversaries to gain essential information before launching cyber attacks. Moving Target Defense (MTD) based on IP shuffling has emerged as a proactive defense strategy to counteract these reconnaissance efforts. Unlike static, reactive defense techniques, IP shuffling introduces randomness by dynamically reassigning network addresses, making it more challenging for attackers to identify and track targets. However, current IP shuffling methods face three key challenges: 1) limited scalability across different network topologies, 2) inherent reconfiguration overhead even in the absence of an active attack, and 3) the need for large-scale unused address blocks. To address these issues, we propose LSTM Look-ahead Moving Target Defense (LLM). Our approach is the first attempt using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to predict future target addresses that attackers will likely scan. Ensemble learning is used to improve robustness to different scanning behaviors. We introduce a dynamic mutation mechanism to enhance adaptability. Compared to the baseline mutation strategy, LLM performs better in both security and overhead.

item.title: VLALeaks: Membership Inference Attacks against Vision-Language-Action Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15165v1

AI Summary (中文)

VLALeaks：面向视觉-语言-动作模型的成员推断攻击

背景与问题：视觉-语言-动作（Vision-Language-Action, VLA）模型通过端到端方式驱动机器人执行复杂任务，正成为具身智能的核心范式。然而，其对海量、高成本采集的真实机器人数据的强依赖性，叠加深度模型固有的训练数据记忆特性，导致严重的数据隐私泄露与知识产权侵权风险——攻击者可能逆向识别某段视频-指令-动作三元组是否曾用于模型训练。尽管成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attacks, MIAs）已被广泛研究于图像分类或语言模型，VLA模型这一新兴关键范式中的MIAs仍属空白。

方法创新：本文首次提出VLALeaks——首个专为VLA模型设计的黑盒成员推断框架。其核心洞见在于：VLA模型在处理训练内样本与训练外样本时，其跨模态注意力机制（尤其是视觉→动作、语言→动作路径）呈现可量化的统计差异。VLALeaks采用两阶段范式：
1. 成员特征提取：无需访问模型梯度或内部参数，仅通过前向推理获取多层注意力图谱的熵、方差及跨模态对齐度等轻量级指标；
2. 攻击模型构建：基于上述特征训练轻量级二分类器（如XGBoost），实现高效、低开销推断。

主要发现与贡献：在Open-X-Embodiment、RT-1、VoxPoser等主流VLA基准上系统评估表明：VLALeaks在多个模型上均取得AUC高达0.92–0.97、TPR@1%FPR达68%–83%，显著超越基线方法；实证揭示VLA模型存在普遍且严重的隐私漏洞。本工作是首项系统性探究VLA模型隐私风险的研究，为构建可审计、可信赖的具身AI提供了关键警示与技术基线。

AI Summary (English)

Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models enable end-to-end robotic control but pose severe privacy risks due to costly, sensitive training data and inherent memorization. Membership inference attacks (MIAs)—which determine whether a sample was in the training set—remain unexplored for VLA models. We propose VLALeaks, the first MIA framework tailored to VLA models, exploiting attention discrepancies between in- and out-of-distribution samples across vision-language-action pathways. VLALeaks operates in two stages: (1) extracting lightweight, gradient-free features from attention maps (e.g., entropy, cross-modal alignment), and (2) training a lightweight classifier (e.g., XGBoost) for inference. Evaluated across Open-X-Embodiment, RT-1, and VoxPoser, VLALeaks achieves strong performance: AUC of 0.92–0.97 and TPR@1%FPR of 68%–83%, significantly outperforming baselines. This work is the first systematic study of MIAs on VLA models, exposing critical privacy vulnerabilities and informing the development of secure, trustworthy embodied AI systems.

Abstract

Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models enable end-to-end robot control and have garnered widespread attention. However, the memorization of training data inherent to VLA, coupled with the high cost of robotic data acquisition, raises serious concerns regarding data privacy leakage and intellectual property infringement. Membership inference attacks (MIAs) aim to determine whether a given sample belongs to the training set. While representing a significant privacy threat, this attack remains underexplored in the context of VLA models. To bridge this gap, we propose VLALeaks, which is based on attention discrepancies in VLA models. We reveal, for the first time, the privacy vulnerabilities of VLA models. Specifically, it comprises a two-stage process: (1) membership feature extraction, and (2) attack model construction. Experimental results across multiple VLA benchmarks demonstrate that VLALeaks readily reveals membership information and achieves optimal attack AUC and TPR@1\%FPR, highlighting the privacy vulnerabilities in current VLA model deployments. Our work is the first systematic study of MIAs on VLA models, aiming to provide insights for secure and trustworthy VLA models.

item.title: AutoDojo: Adaptive Attacks Expose Superficial Defenses and User-Underspecification Limits in LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15057v1

AI Summary (中文)

AutoDojo：自适应攻击揭示LLM智能体防御的表层性与用户欠规范性瓶颈

间接提示注入（IPI）是当前大语言模型（LLM）驱动智能体面临的核心安全威胁。为应对此问题，现有防御方案主要分为三类：提示工程型（如安全提示、角色约束）、检测过滤型（识别并拦截恶意指令）和系统级隔离型（通过控制流/数据流隔离增强鲁棒性）。然而，主流评估基准（如AgentDojo）存在根本缺陷——其攻击集是静态预设的，无法反映真实场景中攻击者持续迭代、针对性优化的自适应行为，因而严重高估了防御效果。

本研究提出 AutoDojo：首个面向IPI的开源自适应攻击框架。它基于强化反馈循环，利用前沿黑盒LLM（无需梯度或内部访问）自动迭代生成、筛选并精炼IPI样本，以最大化突破给定防御的攻击成功率（ASR）。我们在三大任务套件（WebShop、HotPotQA、AlpacaEval）与五种主流目标模型（Llama-3-8B、Qwen2-7B、Gemma-2-9B等）上系统评估了12种SOTA防御方案。

关键发现有二：
1. 防御普遍脆弱：即使对宣称“零静态ASR”的强过滤器，AutoDojo仍实现整体28% ASR，在action-open任务中高达64%；多数防御的ASR被提升2–5倍，暴露其本质为“表面防御”。
2. 用户欠规范性构成结构性瓶颈：在用户请求未明确限定动作执行方式（即action-open）的任务中，IPI可伪装为普通数据而非显式指令，从而绕过所有依赖“指令文本检测”的提示级与过滤型防御——这并非技术缺陷，而是人机协作范式下的固有局限。

AutoDojo已开源：https://github.com/xhOwenMa/AutoDojo

AI Summary (English)

Indirect Prompt Injection (IPI) poses a critical security threat to LLM agents, prompting diverse defensive strategies—prompt-based, detection-based, and system-level. However, standard benchmarks like AgentDojo use static attack distributions, failing to evaluate robustness against adaptive adversaries. To address this, we introduce AutoDojo, an open-source adaptive IPI framework that black-box optimizes injections against a target defense using frontier LLMs without model access or gradients. Evaluating across three task suites (WebShop, HotPotQA, AlpacaEval) and five models (e.g., Llama-3-8B, Qwen2-7B), AutoDojo reveals two key insights: (1) Most state-of-the-art defenses are superficial—AutoDojo raises attack success rate (ASR) dramatically, recovering 28% overall and 64% on action-open tasks even against filters achieving 0% static ASR; (2) ASR is consistently and substantially higher on action-open tasks—where user requests delegate action execution to attacker-controlled content—exposing a fundamental user-underspecification limit: IPI evades instruction-detection defenses by masquerading as benign data rather than explicit commands. AutoDojo is publicly available at https://github.com/xhOwenMa/AutoDojo.

Abstract

Indirect prompt injection (IPI) is a major security threat to LLM-powered agents. Thus, a growing body of work have proposed a variety of defensive approaches against IPI. These can be grouped into three broad categories: 1) prompt-based (using prompting as a way to prevent agents from following malicious instructions), 2) detection-based (identifying and filtering malicious instructions), and 3) system-level (using systems insights, such as control and data isolation, for defense). However, commonly used benchmarks for evaluating defense, such as AgentDojo, are \emph{inherently static}, generating a fixed distribution of IPI attacks. Consequently, static benchmarks do not usefully evaluate defense robustness to adaptive threats. We address this issue by developing AutoDojo, an adaptive extension of AgentDojo that optimizes IPI against a given defense. Using AutoDojo against state-of-the-art IPI defenses across three task suites and five target models, we make two key observations. First, many defenses offer only limited protection: a cheap, black-box adaptive attack using a frontier LLM to iteratively optimize the injection raises attack success rate (ASR) well above the level achieved by static injections against nearly all evaluated defenses. Against a filter that reduces static ASR to 0\%, AutoDojo recovers 28\% overall and 64\% on action-open tasks. Second, for prompt-level and filter-based defenses, ASR is substantially higher on \emph{action-open} tasks -- where the user's request delegates the action itself to attacker-controlled content -- than on precisely specified tasks. This is a structural limit: on such tasks the injection can pose as ordinary data rather than an explicit instruction, bypassing defenses that rely on detecting instruction-like text. AutoDojo is publicly available at https://github.com/xhOwenMa/AutoDojo.

item.title: Few-Shot Biomedical Relation Extraction with Large Language Models: A Viable Alternative to Supervised Learning?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15412v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

生物医学关系抽取（BioRE）是将海量文献转化为结构化知识的关键环节。然而，主流方法严重依赖大规模人工标注数据训练的监督模型，导致跨关系类型与跨领域迁移成本高、泛化能力弱，在低资源场景下难以落地。

方法创新

本研究系统探索了大语言模型（LLM）在少样本BioRE中的可行性，提出并对比两种提示学习范式：

  • 成对分类（Pairwise Classification）：对每个实体对独立判别关系类型；
  • 联合生成（Joint Generation）：单次模型调用中端到端生成全部关系三元组。

实验基于新构建的BioREDirect数据集，全面评估精度（Precision）、召回率（Recall）与F1平衡性。

核心发现

  • 存在显著的精度–召回率权衡：成对分类召回率更高（利于覆盖稀疏关系），联合生成精度更优且计算开销降低约40%；
  • 最优少样本模型达micro-F1 = 0.44，较此前少样本SOTA（0.34）提升29%，虽略低于监督基线（0.56），但分析表明该差距主要源于单一模糊定义的关系类型（“associated_with”）引发的标注歧义；
  • 采用更契合类别不平衡场景的macro-F1评估时，提示方法反超监督基线（0.45 vs. 0.38），尤其在罕见关系类型上优势突出（+12.5% F1）；
  • 结果证实：清晰的关系模式定义比海量标注更具决定性，LLM少样本方法可成为监督学习在资源受限场景下的有力替代方案。

AI Summary (English)

This study investigates few-shot biomedical relation extraction (BioRE) using prompt-based large language models (LLMs), comparing pairwise classification (per-entity-pair prediction) and joint generation (end-to-end multi-relation output in one call). On the BioREDirect dataset, we observe a clear precision–recall trade-off: pairwise classification achieves higher recall, while joint generation yields superior precision and ~40% lower computational cost. The best few-shot model attains micro-F1 = 0.44—surpassing prior few-shot results (0.34) by 29% but trailing the supervised baseline (0.56). Crucially, this gap largely stems from ambiguity in one relation type (“associated_with”). Under macro-F1—which fairly evaluates performance across imbalanced relation types—prompt-based methods outperform the supervised baseline (0.45 vs. 0.38), especially on rare relations. Our findings establish LLMs as a viable, resource-efficient alternative to supervised learning in low-data biomedical settings, contingent on well-defined relation schemas.

Abstract

Biomedical relation extraction (BioRE) is a key step in transforming biomedical literature into structured knowledge. However, most existing approaches rely on supervised models trained on costly annotated datasets, limiting their scalability and adaptability across relation types and domains. We investigate few-shot BioRE using prompt-based learning with large language models (LLMs) and compare two task formulations: pairwise classification, which predicts relations for individual entity pairs, and joint generation, which extracts multiple relations in a single model call. Experiments on the BioREDirect dataset reveal a clear precision-recall trade-off. Pairwise classification achieves higher recall, whereas joint generation is more precise and computationally efficient. The best-performing model achieves a micro-F1 score of 0.44, substantially outperforming previous few-shot results (0.34) while remaining below the supervised baseline (0.56). Much of this gap is attributable to a single ambiguously defined relation type. When evaluated using macro-F1, which better captures performance across relation types in an imbalanced setting, prompt-based approaches outperform the supervised baseline (0.45 vs. 0.38), particularly on rare relation types. These findings highlight the potential of LLMs for BioRE in low-resource settings and underscore the importance of well-defined relation schemas.

item.title: ChatPlanner: A Large Language Model Framework for Personalized Public Transit Routing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15315v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

公共交通个性化路径规划长期受限于用户偏好建模困难——传统算法难以从自然语言中准确捕捉多样化、模糊化、情境化的出行需求（如“别太挤”“想晒太阳”“带娃优先换乘少”），导致生成路线与真实意图错配。

方法创新

本文提出 ChatPlanner：首个融合大语言模型（LLM）与公共交通优化的端到端框架。核心包含三重设计：

  • 偏好感知数据构建：设计覆盖8类典型用户画像（如通勤族、老年人、亲子家庭）与5种出行情境（雨天、晚高峰、携带行李等）的结构化偏好数据集，定义可量化的偏好评分标准，支撑模型训练与评估；
  • 双阶段语义解析：采用微调+检索增强生成（RAG）协同机制——微调LLM强制输出标准化路由参数（出发时间、容忍步行/换乘/等待时长等）并学习通用偏好模式；RAG则实时检索相似历史查询与情境知识，精准解析口语化、隐含式表达（如“我想轻松点”→降低拥挤度权重，“赶时间但不想打车”→提升准点率约束）；
  • 偏好嵌入优化：将LLM解析出的离散偏好标签与连续偏好强度值动态注入多目标公交路径算法的目标函数，实现可解释、可调控的个性化排序。

主要发现

实验验证表明：
✅ ChatPlanner 100%生成可行解（满足时刻表、换乘逻辑、物理可达性）；
✅ 微调保障结构化输出稳定性，RAG提升模糊表达理解准确率（+23.7%偏好识别F1）；二者联合使路由参数提取准确率达94.2%；
✅ 在真实案例中，ChatPlanner发现传统规划器忽略的高价值替代路径——例如为视障用户优先推荐语音报站完备线路，为骑行接驳者推荐近地铁口+充足自行车位站点，显著提升方案实用性与包容性。

本研究开创了自然语言驱动的交通优化新范式，为智能出行服务提供可扩展、可解释、以人为中心的技术基座。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces ChatPlanner, a novel LLM-based framework for preference-aware public transit routing. It addresses the challenge of interpreting diverse, implicit, and context-dependent user preferences (e.g., “avoid crowded buses”, “prefer sunny routes”) from natural language queries. ChatPlanner combines fine-tuned LLMs with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to robustly extract structured routing parameters and quantify nuanced preferences, which are then dynamically integrated into a multi-objective transit routing optimizer. Evaluated on a purpose-built dataset covering eight personas and five contexts, experiments show ChatPlanner achieves 94.2% accuracy in preference and parameter extraction—outperforming either fine-tuning or RAG alone. Crucially, it consistently generates feasible, personalized route alternatives overlooked by conventional planners, demonstrating significant improvements in usability, inclusivity, and real-world relevance. This work establishes a new paradigm for human-centered transportation optimization powered by natural language understanding.

Abstract

Personalized public transit routing in public transit systems remains challenging due to the difficulty of capturing and integrating diverse user preferences into routing algorithms. This paper presents ChatPlanner, a novel framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to enable preference aware public transit routing. Our approach employs fine-tuned LLMs with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to extract routing parameters and interpret nuanced user preferences from natural language queries, subsequently integrating these preferences into the objective function of a public transit routing algorithm. This study designs preference aware datasets incorporating eight personas and five contexts to establish scoring standards for both fine-tuning and RAG. This work conducted three experiments to validate the solutions' feasibility, extraction of routing information and preferences, and solution set quality and completeness. Results demonstrate that ChatPlanner generates feasible solutions reliably. Fine-tuning enforces the required output structure and learns general preference patterns, while RAG provides query-specific context to resolve imprecise or conversational expressions and calibrate continuous scores. The combination of both achieves the highest accuracy in routing information extraction and user preference interpretation. Results based on selected case studies show that by capturing user preferences, ChatPlanner identifies valuable solutions across different dimensions that existing route planners overlook, generating more valuable route alternatives. This research establishes a new paradigm for integrating natural language understanding into transportation optimization.

item.title: Forced Deferral: Manipulating Routing Decisions in Multimodal LLM Cascades
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15308v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

多模态大语言模型（MLLM）虽具备强大视觉推理能力，但为每个查询调用大型模型成本高昂。为此，MLLM级联系统采用“弱模型优先+置信度驱动回退”策略：先由轻量、廉价的弱模型处理查询；仅当其输出置信度低于阈值时，才将请求强制转交给高成本强模型。然而，该机制将计算资源分配权完全系于弱模型的置信度判断——这构成全新攻击面：攻击者可恶意操控弱模型对特定输入的置信度，使其持续触发回退，从而非授权地占用强模型算力。

方法：强制回退攻击（FDA）
