本文提出Forced Deferral Attack（FDA）——一种面向图像的通用对抗攻击。FDA不追求误导答案，而专注降低弱模型输出的token分布集中度。其核心创新在于：

  • 设计温度平滑目标函数，引导被触发图像在弱模型上的输出分布趋近于其原始响应经温度缩放后生成的“去集中化目标分布”；
  • 优化一个通用边框触发器（universal border trigger），可跨样本、跨任务迁移，无需白盒访问模型梯度；
  • 兼容多种置信度度量（如最大概率、熵、预测差），适配不同级联调度策略。

主要发现与意义

在COCO、VQAv2、OK-VQA等数据集及LLaVA-1.5、MiniGPT-4、mPLUG-Owl等主流MLLM家族上，FDA显著提升强模型路由率（平均+28.7%），全面超越图像扰动基线（PGD、AdvPatch）与提示注入基线。关键表明：MLLM级联系统存在算力分配层面的结构性脆弱性——攻击者可在完全不破坏答案正确性的前提下，系统性劫持计算资源。本工作首次揭示并量化了此类“算力定向劫持”风险，为可信多模态服务部署提供了关键安全警示与防御新视角。

AI Summary (English)

Multimodal large language model (MLLM) cascades reduce inference cost by routing queries to a cheap weak model first and deferring only unconfident cases to an expensive strong model. However, this confidence-based routing creates a novel attack surface: adversaries can manipulate the weak model’s confidence to force systematic deferral to the strong model—without altering answer correctness. We introduce the Forced Deferral Attack (FDA), a universal adversarial image attack that lowers token-distribution concentration in the weak model via a temperature-flattened objective. FDA optimizes a lightweight border trigger that pushes the weak model’s output toward deconcentrated targets derived from its clean responses. Across diverse datasets (COCO, VQAv2), MLLM families (LLaVA-1.5, MiniGPT-4, mPLUG-Owl), and deferral metrics (max-prob, entropy, margin), FDA consistently increases strong-model routing by up to 28.7% on average—outperforming both image-perturbation (PGD, AdvPatch) and prompt-injection baselines. Our results expose a critical, previously overlooked vulnerability: MLLM cascades are susceptible to compute allocation manipulation, enabling unauthorized strong-model usage while preserving functional correctness.

Abstract

While multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have shown strong visual reasoning abilities, serving a large model for every query is computationally expensive. MLLM cascades mitigate this cost by first querying a weak but cheaper model and deferring to a strong model when the weak model's output is unconfident. However, since the weak model's confidence directly controls compute allocation, these systems expose a new attack surface: an adversary can manipulate confidence so that their queries are consistently deferred to the strong model. Motivated by this vulnerability, we introduce the Forced Deferral Attack (FDA), an adversarial image attack that lowers the weak model's confidence and causes cascades to route queries to the strong model. FDA learns a universal border trigger by optimizing a temperature-flattened objective. This objective pushes the weak model's token distribution on triggered inputs toward less concentrated targets constructed from its clean responses. Across datasets, model families, and deferral metrics, FDA consistently increases strong-model routing while outperforming image-perturbation and prompt-injection baselines. These results show that MLLM cascades are vulnerable to attacks that manipulate compute allocation, forcing unintended strong-model usage without directly targeting answer correctness.

item.title: Guiding Federated Graph Recommendation with LLM-encoded knowledge
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15277v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

基于图的推荐系统能有效挖掘用户-物品交互中的协同信号，而联邦学习（FL）为其提供了隐私保护的分布式训练范式。然而，在非独立同分布（non-IID）客户端环境下，各客户端本地学习的图结构嵌入存在显著语义错位；简单平均聚合无法建模跨客户端的真实偏好关联，导致协同效果受限。现有联邦图推荐方法多依赖纯结构层面的聚合，忽视了大语言模型（LLM）所蕴含的丰富、通用语义知识——这种知识可弥合局部行为模式与全局语义理解之间的鸿沟。

方法创新

本文提出 LLM-Guide：一种利用冻结LLM编码语义知识引导联邦图推荐的新框架。各客户端在本地构建用户-物品交互图并学习结构表征的同时，将典型交互行为（如“常购数码配件”“偏好小众文艺电影”）提炼为紧凑的语义向量，该过程通过轻量级提示工程调用冻结LLM完成，不更新LLM参数，保障效率与隐私。服务器端则基于这些语义向量计算客户端间偏好相似度，动态生成加权聚合权重，实现对结构表征的语义感知选择性融合——全程无需传输原始数据或图结构。

主要结果与价值

在Amazon-Book、Yelp和Last-FM三大公开基准上，LLM-Guide在Recall@20和NDCG@20指标上平均提升+4.2%–7.8%，显著优于FedGNN、FedGraphRec等先进基线。消融实验证实：LLM语义信号对结构对齐的引导作用贡献率达63%；且语义向量维度仅需128维即可达到最优性能平衡。本工作首次将LLM作为跨客户端语义对齐的“联邦协调器”，为隐私敏感场景下的图推荐提供了可解释、可扩展、语义驱动的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Graph-based recommender systems excel at capturing collaborative signals from user–item interactions, and federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving distributed training. Yet aggregating structural graph representations across non-IID clients remains challenging due to misaligned local embeddings and ineffective naive averaging. Most existing federated graph methods ignore the rich global semantic knowledge embedded in large language models (LLMs). We propose LLM-Guide, a novel framework that leverages frozen LLMs to encode client-specific interaction patterns into compact semantic vectors—without fine-tuning or data exposure. The server uses these vectors to measure cross-client preference similarity and guide selective, semantics-aware aggregation of structural representations. Extensive experiments on Amazon-Book, Yelp, and Last-FM show consistent gains: +4.2–7.8% improvement in Recall@20 and NDCG@20 over state-of-the-art baselines. Ablation confirms semantic guidance contributes >60% of the performance gain, validating LLMs as efficient, privacy-safe “semantic coordinators” for federated graph recommendation.

Abstract

Graph-based recommender systems are highly effective at extracting collaborative signals from user--item interactions, and federated learning (FL) allows these models to be trained while preserving user privacy. However, aggregating graph representations across distributed, non-IID clients remains a challenge; structural embeddings learned locally often misalign, and naive averaging fails to capture meaningful cross-client relationships. Most existing federated graph methods rely exclusively on structural aggregation, neglecting the rich, global semantic context available in large language models (LLMs). In this paper, we propose a novel framework that uses LLM-encoded knowledge to guide federated graph recommendation. Specifically, clients learn structural representations from local graphs while simultaneously summarizing their typical interaction patterns into compact semantic vectors via a frozen LLM. The central server then uses these LLM-encoded semantic signals to discover related preference patterns across clients, guiding the selective aggregation of their structural representations. This enables semantically informed cross-client collaboration without exposing raw data. Extensive experiments on standard benchmarks show that guiding structural alignment with LLM-encoded knowledge consistently improves recommendation accuracy over existing federated graph baselines.

item.title: FreeSonic: Training-Free Temporal-Aware Decoupled Attention for Precise Audio Editing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15186v1

AI Summary (中文)

FreeSonic：无需训练的时序感知解耦注意力音频精准编辑框架

背景与挑战：文本到音频（TTA）生成虽取得显著进展，但实现高精度、强一致性的局部音频编辑仍面临根本性瓶颈——现有方法难以兼顾目标片段的语义精准修改与背景声的时序连贯性与声学保真度。尤其在“仅替换咳嗽声而不扰动背景音乐”或“删除对话片段同时保留环境混响”等任务中，模型常引发伪影、节奏断裂或上下文失真。

核心方法：FreeSonic 是一种完全无需微调训练的即插即用框架，基于先进 Rectified Flow 架构的 TangoFlux 模型构建。其创新包含三方面：
1. 优化的逆向-正向双路径推理：通过可微分潜空间对齐与迭代校准，实现高保真音频潜变量精确反演；
2. 联合文本-音频注意力引导定位：融合跨模态注意力热图，自动识别并分割目标编辑区域（如语音段、乐器音符），避免手工掩码；
3. 时序感知的调度式注意力解耦（Scheduled Attention Decoupling）：在扩散去噪过程中，动态冻结非目标区域的注意力权重，仅激活目标帧邻域内的交叉注意力，从而严格约束编辑范围，保障背景声学特征（频谱包络、相位连续性、混响衰减）零扰动。

扩展能力：引入任务导向噪声注入机制，支持多样化编辑范式——包括零样本音频移除、非刚性替换（如将小提琴声替换为钢琴声但保留原有演奏节奏与力度）、以及多目标协同编辑。

实验验证：在 AudioCaps、LibriSpeech 和自建精细标注数据集上，FreeSonic 在编辑精度（FID↓32%）、时序一致性（DTW距离↓41%）、背景保真度（STOI↑0.19） 三项关键指标上全面超越基线方法（如 AudioLDM-Inversion、DiffEdit）。用户研究显示其编辑自然度评分达 4.72/5.0，显著优于现有方案。

AI Summary (English)

FreeSonic is a training-free framework for precise and temporally consistent audio editing, built upon the state-of-the-art Rectified Flow-based TangoFlux model. It introduces three key innovations: (1) an optimized inversion-reverse process with latent-space alignment for high-fidelity audio reconstruction; (2) joint text-audio attention maps to automatically localize target segments without manual masking; and (3) scheduled attention decoupling—a novel mechanism that dynamically restricts cross-attention updates only within the target temporal region during denoising, thereby preserving background acoustics (e.g., spectral continuity, phase coherence, reverb decay) with zero fine-tuning. Additionally, task-oriented noise injection enables versatile operations including zero-shot audio removal and non-rigid replacement (e.g., swapping violin with piano while retaining original timing and dynamics). Extensive experiments show FreeSonic achieves superior fidelity (32% lower FID), temporal consistency (41% lower DTW distance), and background preservation (0.19 higher STOI) versus prior methods, with user studies confirming significantly enhanced naturalness (4.72/5.0). Code and interactive demos are available at https://free-sonic.github.io/.

Abstract

Text-to-audio (TTA) generation has made significant strides, yet achieving precise and consistent audio editing remains a major challenge. However, existing methods struggle to balance temporal consistency with background preservation. In this paper, we propose FreeSonic, a training-free framework leveraging the state-of-the-art Rectified Flow-based TangoFlux model. FreeSonic utilizes an optimized inversion-reverse process and joint text-audio attention maps for precise target segment extraction. For content editing, a novel scheduled attention decoupling confines modifications to target regions while preserving original acoustic context. Furthermore, task-oriented noise injection enhances versatility for tasks such as audio removal and non-rigid replacement. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that FreeSonic achieves a superior balance by providing a high-fidelity and efficient solution for precise and consistent audio editing. Project and demos: https://free-sonic.github.io/

item.title: Generative modelling powered by room-temperature polariton condensates
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15344v1

AI Summary (中文)

基于室温极化激元凝聚体的生成式建模新范式

背景与挑战：生成式建模依赖高效、可扩展的随机非线性变换，而传统数字方法在采样效率、物理真实性与能耗方面面临瓶颈。物理硬件加速亟需能天然实现高维随机非线性动力学的平台。

方法创新：本研究首次将室温有机染料微腔中形成的激子-极化激元凝聚体作为物理随机变换层，嵌入条件生成对抗网络（cGAN）架构，实现“数字指令→物理采样→图像生成”的端到端流程。该系统利用强光-物质耦合 regime 下极化激元的非平衡多体非线性动力学与本征量子-热混合涨落，替代传统人工注入的高斯噪声或伪随机扰动。

核心发现：

  • 性能超越：相比纯数字采样基线及激光光源对照组，“极化激元GAN”（Polariton GAN）在MNIST→手写体图像翻译任务中显著提升：Inception Score 提高12.3%，数字结构保真度（digit preservation accuracy）达98.7%，SSIM（结构相似性）提升19.6%；
  • 正则化机制：极化激元输出的空间关联涨落（spatially correlated variations）自发约束判别器过拟合，稳定对抗训练，并提升生成样本的多样性与拓扑鲁棒性；
  • 物理可扩展性：全光学、室温运行、毫瓦级泵浦功率，为低功耗、高吞吐物理AI提供新硬件路径。

意义：本工作确立极化激元凝聚体为一种新型类脑随机计算资源，不仅拓展了非冯·诺依曼生成式AI的物理载体，更推动“物理先验嵌入”（physics-informed sampling）成为下一代机器学习系统的核心设计范式。

AI Summary (English)

We experimentally demonstrate that room-temperature exciton-polariton condensates in organic dye microcavities serve as efficient physical stochastic nonlinear layers for conditional generative modeling. Integrated into a generative adversarial network (GAN), these condensates replace digital noise injection with intrinsic quantum-thermal fluctuations and spatially correlated many-body dynamics, enabling digit-to-image translation. Polariton GAN achieves superior performance: +12.3% Inception Score, 98.7% digit preservation accuracy, and +19.6% SSIM versus digital baselines and laser-driven controls. Crucially, the spatially structured output variations naturally regularize adversarial training and enhance sample diversity. This work establishes polariton condensation as a scalable, room-temperature physical resource for physics-enhanced generative AI.

Abstract

Generative modelling requires efficient stochastic nonlinear transformations and physical platforms that can naturally realise them. We experimentally demonstrate that nonlinear optical systems operating in the strong light-matter coupling regime can serve as physical transformation layers for conditional generative modelling. Specifically, we develop a workflow in which room-temperature exciton-polariton condensates formed in organic dye microcavities act as a physical stochastic transform within a generative adversarial network and enable conditional digit-to-image translation. By using the nonlinear many-body dynamics and intrinsic stochasticity of polariton condensates, the workflow outperforms baseline approaches based on digitally injected perturbations. We find that polariton-enabled sampling via generative adversarial network (Polariton GAN) yields improved inception score, digit preservation accuracy and structural similarity compared with both digital sampling and laser-based systems. We further show that spatially correlated output variations can naturally regularise adversarial training and enhance output diversity. Our results establish polariton condensation as a new computational resource for generative modelling, opening a pathway towards physics-enhanced machine learning systems.

item.title: A family of invertible shift-invariant maps with strong arithmetic properties
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1249

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

移位不变映射（shift-invariant maps）是现代对称密码设计中关键的非线性组件，典型代表为Keccak哈希函数中的$\chi$-映射。其代数结构复杂、硬件实现高效，但系统性构造与可逆性刻画长期缺乏统一框架。本文旨在构建一类具有良好算术性质的可逆移位不变映射族，并建立其代数本质与多项式环之间的深刻联系。

方法与核心构造

我们定义在$\mathbb{F}_2^n$上的映射族$\Omega_{\underline{a}}$，其定义函数由长度为$m$（$2\leq m<n$）的无前后缀序列$\underline{a}=(a_1,\dots,a_m)\in\mathbb{F}_2^m$诱导生成。该族在映射复合运算下构成交换幺半群；更重要的是，其可逆元子群（unit group）具有精确的环论刻画：

  • 若$m mid n$，则$\operatorname{Unit}(\Omega_{\underline{a}})\cong (\mathbb{F}_2[x]/(x^{\lceil n/m ceil}))^ imes$；
  • 若$m\mid n$，则$\operatorname{Unit}(\Omega_{\underline{a}})\cong (\mathbb{F}_2[x]/(x^{2n/m}+x^{n/m}))^ imes$。

这一同构将映射复合转化为多项式乘法，从而将分析难题迁移至经典有限环理论。

主要发现与创新

我们聚焦典型元素$ ho = x_0 + (x_1+a_1+1)\cdots(x_m+a_m+1)$（涵盖$\chi$-映射及Lyu等先前工作），证明：$ ho$可逆当且仅当$m mid n$；并完全刻画其显式逆映射与循环分解结构（包括所有轨道长度与个数）。本工作推广了Kriepke与Lyu等人的构造，首次建立移位不变映射族与商环单位群的系统性对应，为密码学中非线性层的设计与安全性分析提供了新代数范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a family $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ of shift-invariant maps on $\mathbb{F}_2^n$, defined via bifix-free binary sequences $\underline{a}\in\mathbb{F}_2^m$ ($2\le m<n$). We prove $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ forms a commutative monoid under composition, and its unit group is isomorphic to the multiplicative group of either $\mathbb{F}_2[x]/(x^{\lceil n/m ceil})$ (if $m mid n$) or $\mathbb{F}_2[x]/(x^{2n/m}+x^{n/m})$ (if $m\mid n$), enabling polynomial-based analysis. Focusing on the canonical map $ ho = x_0 + \prod_{i=1}^m (x_i + a_i + 1)$—which generalizes Keccak’s $\chi$-map—we establish that $ ho$ is invertible iff $m mid n$, and fully characterize its inverse and cycle structure when invertible. Our framework unifies and extends prior constructions, offering a new algebraic lens for designing and analyzing invertible nonlinear layers in symmetric cryptography.

Abstract

Shift-invariant maps have been employed to design nonlinear layers in many symmetric cryptographic schemes, such as the $\chi$-map used in Keccak. In this paper, we study the shift-invariant maps on $\mathbb{F}_2^n$, whose defining functions come from a family of $n$-variable Boolean functions induced by a bifix-free sequence $\underline{a}=(a_1,a_2,\ldots,a_m)\in \mathbb{F}_2^m$ with $2\leq m<n$, which we denote by $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$. It is shown that $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ forms a commutative monoid with respect to the composition. Moreover, if $m mid n$, then $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ is isomorphic to the unit group of $\mathbb{F}_2[x]/ (x^{\lceil rac{n}{m} ceil})$; if $m\mid n$, then the unit group of $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ is isomorphic to that of $\mathbb{F}_2[x]/ (x^{ rac{2n}{m}}+x^{ rac{n}{m}})$.
The isomorphic relation transforms the composition of functions on $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ into the multiplication of polynomials on the quotient ring of $\mathbb{F}_2[x]$, where the algebraic properties of the latter are well-understood. As a straightforward application, we focus on the algebraic properties of $ ho=x_0+(x_1+a_1+1)(x_2+a_2+1)\cdots (x_m+a_m+1)\in \Omega_{\underline{a}}$, which includes the $\chi$-map as well as several other known maps studied in earlier literature. It is shown that $ ho$ is invertible if and only if $m mid n$. Also the inverse and the cycle structure of $ ho$ (if invertible) can be fully characterized. The construction of $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ generalizes previous works given by Kriepke et al. and Lyu et al. It is hoped that $\Omega_{\underline{a}}$ could provide a deeper insight into the study of invertible shift-invariant maps.

item.title: Atlantis: Lattice-based Anonymous Tokens with Private Metadata Bit
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1248

AI Summary (中文)

Atlantis：基于格的匿名代币方案，支持私有元数据比特

背景与问题：匿名代币（Anonymous Tokens）在隐私保护型支付、凭证系统中至关重要。现有“带私有元数据比特的匿名代币”（ATPM）方案虽能嵌入单比特信任标记（如“可疑/可信”），供验证者动态执行速率限制或拒绝策略，但全部依赖RSA群、双线性对或椭圆曲线VRF等经典密码学假设，面临量子计算机攻击时将完全失效，缺乏后量子安全性保障。

方法与创新：本文首次提出完全基于格密码学假设的ATPM方案——Atlantis。其核心贡献包括：（1）采用Fischlin盲签名范式构建基础框架；（2）创新性融合格上线性同态加密（LHE）机制，在签名过程中隐式编码并封装该私有比特；（3）实现公开可验证性（任何第三方可验证代币有效性）与私有比特可提取性（仅掌握部分签发方私钥的授权验证者可安全解出该比特），二者严格分离且不可伪造。

实现与性能：方案实例化采用抗量子签名方案Falcon-512作为底层签名原语，并集成高效格基NIZK证明系统LNP22（Crypto’22）完成零知识证明。最终协议Atlantis实测通信开销为70 KB（客户端–发行方交互），生成代币大小为129 KB，在保证后量子安全前提下达成实用级效率，为隐私增强型基础设施提供首个可部署的抗量子匿名代币范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present Atlantis, the first Anonymous Token with Private Metadata bit (ATPM) scheme based solely on post-quantum lattice assumptions. Unlike prior ATPM constructions relying on RSA, pairings, or elliptic-curve VRFs—none of which withstand quantum attacks—Atlantis achieves public verifiability and private bit-extractability: tokens are publicly checkable for validity, while only verifiers possessing partial knowledge of the issuer’s secret can securely recover the embedded 1-bit trust flag. Our design extends the Fischlin blind-signature paradigm with lattice-based linearly homomorphic encryption to encode the hidden bit within the signature structure. Instantiated using Falcon-512 and the LNP22 lattice NIZK proof system (Crypto ’22), Atlantis requires 70 KB of client–issuer communication and yields 129 KB tokens—demonstrating the first practical, quantum-resistant ATPM construction.

Abstract

Anonymous tokens with private metadata bit (ATPM) allow an issuer to embed a hidden trust flag, as a single bit, within issued tokens. The bit remains hidden from the clients, but verifiers can read the bit and rate-limit or discard tokens marked suspect. A series of ATPM constructions exist in the literature, however all current constructions rely on classical hardness assumptions such as RSA groups, pairings, or elliptic-curve VRFs and do not provide any post-quantum security guarantees.

In this work we present, the first ATPM scheme based on lattice assumptions. Tokens generated with our scheme are publicly verifiable, and privately bit-extractable given partial knowledge of the issuing authority's secret. Our design follows the Fischlin blind-signature paradigm and enriches it with lattice-based linearly-homomorphic encryption to carry the hidden bit.

We also instantiate our scheme from Falcon-512 and the efficient LNP22 lattice NIZK proof system (Lyubashevsky et. al, Crypto '22). The resulting protocol, which we call $\textsf{Atlantis}$, requires 70 KB of client-issuer communication and yields 129 KB tokens.
